WHEREAS, on March 18 2024, the City Council adopted Strive San Mateo General Plan 2040; 
and
WHEREAS, a comprehensive update of the City’s Zoning Code (Municipal Code Title 27) is 
necessary in order to implement the goals, policy and vision of General Plan 2040; and
WHEREAS, in March 2025, a Request for Proposals (RFP) for a Comprehensive Zoning Code 
Update to Implement General Plan 2040 from qualified technical planning consultants was issued; and
WHEREAS, in May 2025, following a review of proposals and follow-up interviews, staff 
identified the proposal from PlaceWorks as being the best suited to support the City’s Comprehensive 
Zoning Code Update project; and
WHEREAS, the proposal from PlaceWorks to support the Comprehensive Zoning Code Update 
project is for an amount of $842,587; and
WHEREAS, a 10% contingency, in the amount of $84,259, is included in the event that 
additional services are required during the course of the project.
NOW, THEREFORE, THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA, HEREBY RESOLVES 
that:
1. This fund appropriation is not a project of CEQA because it is an organizational or 
administrative activity that will not impact the environment (CEQA Guidelines Section 
15378(b)(5).
2. The appropriation of $926,846 from the Advance Planning Fund (Fund 225) to the 
Comprehensive Zoning Code Update project is approved.

mediation.  The parties shall meet in mediation within 30 days of a request.  The mediator shall be 
agreed to by the mediating parties; in the absence of an agreement, the parties shall each submit one 
name from mediators listed by either the American Arbitration Association, the State Mediation and 
Conciliation Service, or other agreed-upon service.  The mediator shall be selected by a blind draw. 
 
 
The cost of mediation shall be borne equally by the parties.  Neither party shall be deemed the 
prevailing party.  No party shall be permitted to file a legal action without first meeting in mediation 
and making a good faith attempt to reach a mediated settlement.  The mediation process, once 
commenced by a meeting with the mediator, shall last until agreement is reached by the parties but 
not more than 60 days, unless the maximum time is extended by the parties.

EXHIBIT C 
 
INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS 
 
MINIMUM SCOPE OF INSURANCE  
Coverage shall be at least as broad as: 
 
1. Commercial General Liability (CGL):  Insurance Services Office (ISO) Form CG 00 01 12 07 
covering CGL on an “occurrence” basis, including products-completed operations, personal & 
advertising injury, with limits no less than $2,000,000 per occurrence. If a general aggregate 
limit applies, either the general aggregate limit shall apply separately to this project/location or 
the general aggregate limit shall be twice the required occurrence limit. 
 
2. Automobile Liability:  ISO Form Number CA 00 01 covering any auto (Code 1), or if 
CONSULTANT has no owned autos, hired, (Code 8) and non-owned autos (Code 9), with limit no 
less than $1,000,000 per accident for bodily injury and property damage. 
 
3. Workers’ Compensation: as required by the State of California, with Statutory Limits, and 
Employer’s Liability Insurance with limit of no less than $1,000,000 per accident for bodily 
injury or disease.  
 
4. Professional Liability (Errors and Omissions): Insurance appropriate to the CONSULTANT’s 
profession, with limit no less than $1,000,000 per occurrence or claim, $2,000,000 aggregate 
 
If the CONSULTANT maintains higher limits than the minimums shown above, the City requires and 
shall be entitled to coverage for the higher limits maintained by the CONSULTANT. 
 
Other Insurance Provisions 
The insurance policies are to contain, or be endorsed to contain, the following provisions: 
 
Additional Insured Status 
The City, its elected and appointed officials, employees, and agents are to be covered as insureds on 
the auto policy for liability arising out of automobiles owned, leased, hired or borrowed by or on behalf 
of the CONSULTANT; and on the CGL policy with respect to liability arising out of work or operations 
performed by or on behalf of the CONSULTANT including materials, parts or equipment furnished in 
connection with such work or operations. General liability coverage can be provided in the form of an 
endorsement to the CONSULTANT’s insurance (at least as broad as ISO Form CG 20 10, 11 85 or both 
CG 20 10 and CG 20 37 forms if later revisions used). 
 
Primary Coverage 
For any claims related to this contract, the CONSULTANT’s insurance coverage shall be primary 
insurance as respects the City, its elected and appointed officials, employees, and agents. Any 
insurance or self-insurance maintained by the City, its elected and appointed officials, employees, or 
agents shall be excess of the CONSULTANT’s insurance and shall not contribute with it.

--- page 191 ---
This chapter describes the scope of services to be completed by the PlaceWorks team for the San Mateo Zoning Code 
Update. To facilitate your review of this proposal, we have prepared a concise scope that emphasizes key 
components of our approach to this project.  
We are flexible regarding the proposed scope of work and will work with you to prepare a more detailed scope when 
we enter into a contract. We also recognize that it may be necessary to alter the scope as the project progresses and 
would be happy to work with you to ensure the successful completion of the project.  
PlaceWorks will meet with City staff to initiate the project via a virtual kick-off meeting. We will review the scope and 
schedule with a focus on the intersection of outreach events and project milestones, keeping in mind the goal of 
phasing the project. This meeting will establish project management procedures, including invoicing terms and 
communication protocols, and data collection approach. In advance of this meeting, the PlaceWorks team will 
prepare a data request document.  
The PlaceWorks project management team will participate in weekly virtual status update meetings with City staff to 
ensure that the project remains on task and budget. Additional PlaceWorks staff will attend meetings as needed. It is 
assumed that the weekly status meetings will be conducted virtually and will average one hour each. PlaceWorks will 
prepare and share a meeting agenda and meeting materials prior to each meeting. PlaceWorks will also participate in 
periodic phone calls with the City’s Project Manager (upon the initiation of either party) on an as-needed basis to 
address any issues that arise between project team meetings.  
The PlaceWorks team will join City staff for virtual progress meetings at important project milestones such as 
completion of certain research and analysis, key community engagement activities, following Planning Commission 
and Council study sessions, and ahead of Planning Commission and City Council adoption hearings. PlaceWorks will 
prepare and share a meeting agenda prior to each meeting. 
PlaceWorks understands that the Strive San Mateo webpage used by the City for the General Plan Update will also be 
used for the Zoning Code Update. PlaceWorks is currently managing the Strive website and will continue to do so to 
provide information about the Zoning Code Update. We will post documents as soon as they are available to the 
public, including summaries of community input, which will be important to keep the community’s trust. The website 
will continue to be usable and functional on all interfaces, including mobile phones, tablets, and computers, and will 
include references/links to City social media accounts.  
PlaceWorks will conduct ongoing project management, including internal coordination, review of project budget, and 
preparation of monthly invoices and progress reports. Amy Sinsheimer will serve as the day-to-day contact for overall 
project management and will oversee coordination of the regular project meetings, budget, and schedule. 
EXHIBIT A and B - Scope of Work and Budget
191 of 1111

--- page 192 ---
Deliverables: 
»
Detailed work program (schedule and timeline)
»
Detailed budget tracking document
»
Agendas and notes for kickoff meeting, weekly check-in calls, and key progress meetings
»
Monthly invoices and progress reports
»
Website mock-up and live versions, plus regular updates and linked documents
PlaceWorks will work closely with staff to continue to collect Zoning Code concerns or issues that should be 
addressed as part of the comprehensive update. Following the assessment of background data, PlaceWorks will 
conduct a review of forward-looking best practices to help guide subsequent Zoning Code update tasks. PlaceWorks 
will also work with City staff to identify potential “best practices” jurisdictions to respond to those issues, in addition 
to PlaceWorks existing database of best practices. This task will also include “deep dives” into six to eight particularly 
complex or challenging topics, such as building height, shared and unbundled parking, use classifications for current 
and future uses, or bird-safe regulations.  
PlaceWorks’ GIS team will develop an internal webmap tool to overlay and compare General Plan Land Uses with 
existing zoning designations at the parcel level. Using the webmap, PlaceWorks will work with City staff to identify 
inconsistent parcels to be updated through the subsequent Zoning Map amendments (Task 5.1). It is our 
understanding that this analysis is expected to identify thousands of parcels in San Mateo where the current zoning 
district no longer matches the new General Plan designations. PlaceWorks will work with City staff to discuss any of 
the parcels identified in the webmap, before City staff responds with recommendations of how to resolve 
inconstancies on each of the parcels. While this analysis will identify inconsistencies, we recognize that rezoning can 
be a sensitive issue for residents and property owners and we anticipate close collaboration with staff to determine 
the smoothest process for notifying residents and explaining these changes.  
PlaceWorks will prepare a table of comparisons, outlining the necessary Zoning Code updates needed to meet 
Transit-Oriented Communities (TOC) Policy requirements for density, housing affordability (if any), parking, station 
access, and circulation. This scope assumes that Metropolitan Transportation Commission’s (MTC’s) TOC policy will be 
substantially similar to the version in place as of April 2025. To remain consistent with General Plan 2040 and cover 
the Zoning Code updates with an addendum as described in Task 4, this scope also assumes that any identified policy 
changes for MTC TOC consistency that might trigger a General Plan Amendment would be addressed through a 
separate process.  
PlaceWorks will review and assess other adopted plans and policies against the adopted Strive 2040 General Plan. The 
adopted plans and policies the PlaceWorks team will review include: 
• Bay Meadows Specific Plan and Amendments
(Phases I and II)
• Detroit Drive Specific Plan
• El Camino Real Master Plan
• Hillsdale Station Area Plan
• Mariner’s Island Specific Plan
• Multifamily and Residential Mixed-Use Objective
Design Standards
• San Mateo Rail Corridor Transit-Oriented
Development Plan
• Shoreline Specific Plan
• City Charter
192 of 1111

--- page 193 ---
For each plan, PlaceWorks will prepare a high-level review of the major plan components, examples of plan provisions 
that conflict with General Plan 2040, and big-picture recommendations for addressing inconsistencies, whether through 
amendments to the other plan or policy, and/or retiring the other plan or policy, if appropriate. The process to complete 
amendments is addressed in Task 7.3.     
Based on input received through the previous tasks, PlaceWorks will prepare a draft framework outline that identifies 
the structure of the code, revisions to the existing code and new provisions to be added. Sections anticipated to be 
focused on in the framework outline include development standards, inclusionary housing requirements, and off-street 
parking regulations. The overall approach to identifying where change is needed will be guided by better addressing 
equity, affordable housing, mobility, community assets, infrastructure, resilience and climate adaptation. We will also 
prepare a template to illustrate the intended format and style of the zoning code. As part of this task, we will ensure 
that the code is set up to be easily accessible, interactive, and searchable. After staff review, PlaceWorks will discuss 
with staff any needed changes. 
In addition to the engagement activities described in this task, Planning Commission and City Council study sessions 
detailed under Task 5.9 will be important sources of input for the project. 
As an initial phase of this task, PlaceWorks will use input from the kick-off meeting to develop a Public Engagement 
Plan for the project. The Public Engagement Plan will provide goals, methods of engagement, and an anticipated 
schedule for outreach components. Keeping in mind the City’s goal to complete this project expeditiously, preliminary 
recommendations for engagement efforts include: 
• Two in-person and two virtual community workshops or open house events
• Up to 12 stakeholder group meetings
• Focused online surveys to informed stakeholders and frequent code users
Each of these components is described in more detail in the following subtasks and may be refined or revised 
following the kick-off meeting. 
In support of the community engagement, PlaceWorks will prepare the following outreach materials: 
• Citywide Mailer. PlaceWorks will prepare a citywide mailer to inform stakeholders about the project, provide a link to
the project website, and publish initial outreach dates as available. This scope of work assumes the City will be
responsible for the mailer printing and postage.
• Flyers. PlaceWorks will prepare up to four project flyers.
• Online Surveys. PlaceWorks will prepare up to four online surveys to seek feedback from frequent zoning code users
to get input on targeted issues the update will tackle.
• Project Mailing List Emails. PlaceWorks will prepare emails to announce upcoming outreach opportunities and public
meetings and other key project milestones.
• Social Media Posts. PlaceWorks will prepare social media posts for the City to update the public about the project.
• City Newsletter Content. PlaceWorks will prepare approximately a half page of content for the Citywide newsletter
providing an update on the Zoning Code process.
All materials will be prepared in English. PlaceWorks will translate the engagement materials into Spanish and 
Simplified Chinese. This scope does not include translation of deliverables from other tasks.   
193 of 1111

--- page 194 ---
PlaceWorks will work with City staff to identify appropriate stakeholders, such as developers, real estate brokers, 
architects, business owners, community groups, government agencies, and property owners, to participate in the 
zoning code update process. We propose up to 12 small-group meetings with invited participants to discuss topics such 
as existing constraints, project feasibility and marketability, potential opportunities, and to review the draft code. 
PlaceWorks will facilitate the stakeholder meetings and will provide support through the preparation of emailed 
invitation language, meeting agendas, and discussion questions for City review. PlaceWorks will coordinate with the 
City to determine if these meetings will be conducted in person or virtually. We assume that the City will be 
responsible for inviting stakeholders to the meetings and managing RSVPs. 
PlaceWorks will facilitate two virtual and two in-person community meetings in the form of an open house or workshop 
that will be held at key points of the planning process. Potential engagement points include introducing the project and 
receiving community feedback on zoning concerns and asking for feedback on the Public Review Draft Zoning Code 
Amendments. For each community meeting, PlaceWorks will prepare draft and final materials, including a meeting 
agenda, sign-in sheet, presentation, open house boards and/or workshop activity, comment cards, and handouts as 
needed. We assume up to three PlaceWorks staff, including one bilingual staff member, for each community meeting 
with additional staffing and interpretation provided by the City. 
PlaceWorks will prepare an engagement summary that recaps all engagement efforts throughout the project. The 
summary will also document key themes and issues that we heard from the community, identify how the project 
responded to community input, and include raw data from outreach events.  
Deliverables: 
»
Draft and Final Public Engagement Plan
»
Citywide Newsletter content
»
Flyers (4)
»
Online Survey (4)
»
Project Emails
»
Social Media Posts
»
Translated outreach materials
»
Community Outreach and Engagement Summary
PlaceWorks will prepare an addendum to the Strive San Mateo General Plan 2040 EIR, herein referred to as the 
“General Plan EIR” to evaluate the comprehensive zoning code update. This proposed approach to prepare an 
addendum to the certified General Plan EIR is based on the presumption that the proposed zoning code update will 
be within the scope of and consistent with the adopted General Plan and would therefore not create any new or 
worsened impacts than those identified in the General Plan EIR. PlaceWorks recommends an addendum, rather than 
a tiered mitigated negative declaration, because an addendum is a more legally defensible CEQA document. The 
addendum process does not involve a scoping or response to comments process. Additionally, because the zoning 
code update is presumed to be consistent with the adopted General Plan, technical analysis is not required. 
PlaceWorks understands that an earlier, text only, phase of high density zone code updates will also be prepared and 
will require CEQA review. This scope of work assumes that City staff will prepare the CEQA documentation (expected 
to be streamlined review under CEQA Guidelines Section 15183) for the high density zone text changes. Therefore, 
this scope of work only covers the latter, comprehensive phase of zoning code updates (Task 5).  
PlaceWorks’ CEQA Lead, Alexis Mena, will participate in a virtual meeting with PlaceWorks’ zoning team and City staff 
to kick off the CEQA phase of the project. The meeting focus will be to familiarize the CEQA team with the contents of 
the zoning code update, and to discuss the next steps and timeline for preparing the addendum. 
194 of 1111

--- page 195 ---
Following the CEQA kick-off meeting, PlaceWorks will prepare a draft project description of the proposed zoning code 
update. PlaceWorks will provide a revised project description based on City comments as part of the Administrative 
Draft Addendum described in Task 4.3. 
PlaceWorks will prepare an addendum to the certified General Plan EIR. The addendum will include a qualitative 
discussion of the proposed zoning code update and substantiate the appropriateness of preparing an addendum for 
the zoning code update, pursuant to CEQA Guidelines Section 15164. The addendum will not include discussions for 
each of the CEQA-required topical areas. A contract modification would be required for any new modeling or major 
quantitative analysis. PlaceWorks will submit the Administrative Draft Addendum electronically (in Word) for City 
review and comment. 
Using one set of consolidated comments from City staff on the Administrative Draft Addendum, PlaceWorks will 
prepare a Screencheck Draft Addendum.  
Using one set of consolidated comments from the City on the Screencheck Draft Addendum, PlaceWorks will prepare 
the Final Addendum. This task assumes that comments received on the Screencheck Draft Addendum will be minimal 
and require minor textual edits or revision. This scope of work also assumes that the Final Addendum will be posted 
online for public review but that a response to comments process will not be required. Therefore, this scope of work 
does not include any responses to comments that the City may receive on the addendum. However, we are available 
to prepare responses to comments and addendum revisions, if requested, for an additional cost. 
Following the approval of the addendum and within five days of approval of the zoning code update, PlaceWorks will 
prepare a Notice of Determination (NOD) for submittal to the County Clerk. PlaceWorks will submit the NOD to the 
Governor’s Office of Land Use and Climate Innovation’s State Clearinghouse. City staff will submit the NOD to the 
County Clerk, pay all applicable filing fees at the time of posting, and post on the City’s website. The budget does not 
include payment of any filing fees.  
Deliverables: 
»
Draft notices for the public and for government agencies
»
Draft project description (Word)
»
Administrative Draft Addendum (Word)
»
Screencheck Draft Addendum (Word and PDF)
»
Final Addendum (PDF)
»
Notice of Determination (PDF)
PlaceWorks proposes to complete the zoning code update in one phase. This approach is intended to meet City goals 
of keep the overall schedule close to two years from initiation to adoption of the zoning amendments. The zoning 
code updates will emphasize user-friendliness for both staff and applicants, adding illustrations, tables, and matrices 
to clarify and simplify requirements and processes.  
195 of 1111

--- page 196 ---
PlaceWorks will prepare the Administrative Draft Zoning Code Amendments for internal review and comment by City 
staff.   
Key amendments will include, but are not limited to: 
• Updates to the zoning map for consistency with the Strive 2040 General Plan land use map and land use designations
• Adding, consolidating, removing, or modifying zoning districts, including for alignment with the General Plan
• Translating stories into building-height limits to align with the General Plan
• Possibly folding overlay district requirements into base zoning districts
• Possibly removing the Bay Meadows Specific Plan zoning district
• Revising development standards for each zoning district
»
Revising development standards in special areas such as Hillsdale Mall, Fashion Island, 25th Avenue, and El
Camino Real
»
Updating floor-area ratio (FAR) for the C1 and C2 districts
»
Updating and consolidating FAR for all zoning districts
»
Updating permitted uses in each zoning district
• Updating parking standards
• Establishing a bird-safe building ordinance
• Establishing a dark sky ordinance
• Updating noise standards
Before preparing the Administrative Draft, PlaceWorks will consult with City staff to determine the appropriate file 
format(s) and preferred tracking procedures to ensure ease of internal use and review. It is assumed that the City will 
provide a single, consolidated set of comments following the review. 
PlaceWorks understands the City is working on other zoning code amendments to be adopted separate from the 
comprehensive zoning code amendments, including, but not limited to, accessory dwelling unit and SB 9 updates, 
historic resources, certain parking requirements, and affordable housing requirements. PlaceWorks expects to 
include those adopted ordinances in the comprehensive zoning code update after they are adopted using the same 
organization as the comprehensive update template and will update cross-referencing as needed at that time.  
Following receipt of consolidated City staff comments, PlaceWorks will prepare a Public Review Draft of the Zoning 
Code Amendments that is suitable to be published on the City’s website.  
Following public review, PlaceWorks will work with City staff to determine edits to make in response to comments 
received on the public review draft zoning code amendments. PlaceWorks will then make revisions and provide the 
Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments to City staff to provide to the Planning Commission and City Council for 
consideration for adoption. The Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments will address any typos or errors and will 
incorporate a summary of edits and responses to comments on the Public Draft from staff, Planning Commission, City 
Council, and the public.  
Following Planning Commission and City Council adoption hearings for the zoning code amendments (see Task 6), 
PlaceWorks will prepare the Final Zoning Code with all revisions in PDF or other preferred format. We assume that 
the City will be responsible for updating the Zoning Code website (e.g., Municode). 
196 of 1111

--- page 197 ---
PlaceWorks will work with City staff to prepare for and attend in-person or virtual study sessions with the Planning 
Commission and City Council at key points in the project. This scope of work assumes up to five total in-person study 
sessions (three Planning Commission and two City Council) with in-person attendance by at least one PlaceWorks 
team member. If virtual study sessions are determined to be preferable to have a greater number of meetings or 
attendance by more PlaceWorks staff, the budget under this task could likely accommodate a larger number of 
meetings. This scope of work assumes that meetings with approval bodies will correspond with project milestones, 
such as providing an overview of the project and engagement plan, to gather input on key issues being considered in 
best practices research and analysis, or at the time of the Public Review Draft. This scope assumes that PlaceWorks 
PIC Joanna Jansen will attend all Planning Commission and City Council study sessions in person.  
Deliverables: 
»
Administrative Draft Zoning Code Amendments
»
Public Review Draft Zoning Code Amendments
»
Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments
»
Final Zoning Code Amendments
»
Attendance at and draft agenda reports and PowerPoint presentations for up to five total Planning Commission
and/or City Council study sessions
PlaceWorks will support City staff with packaging and processing the High Density Zones Code Amendments. 
PlaceWorks will prepare necessary materials for the Planning Commission and City Council, including draft hearing 
notices, draft agenda reports, resolutions, ordinances, presentations, and attachments as needed.  
The PlaceWorks team will attend up to one in-person public hearings each with the Planning Commission and City 
Council at the time of High Density Zones Code Amendments adoption, with in-person attendance by at least one 
PlaceWorks team member. Additional hearings may be added to the scope and reflected in any necessary budget 
augments at the request of City staff.  
PlaceWorks will support City staff with packaging and processing the comprehensive Zoning Code update and Map 
Amendments. PlaceWorks will prepare necessary materials for the Planning Commission and City Council, including 
draft hearing notices, draft agenda reports, resolutions, ordinances, presentations, and attachments as needed.  
The PlaceWorks team will attend up to three in-person public hearings with the Planning Commission and two 
hearings with the City Council at the time of Zoning Code adoption, with in-person attendance by at least one 
PlaceWorks team member. Additional hearings may be added to the scope and reflected in any necessary budget 
augments at the request of City staff.  
Following approval of the comprehensive Zoning Code update, PlaceWorks will file the NOD, as described in Task 4.6. 
197 of 1111

--- page 198 ---
Deliverables: 
»
Attendance at and draft agenda reports, resolutions, ordinances, and PowerPoint presentations for up to four
Planning Commission and four City Council adoption hearings
Following adoption of all Zoning Code Amendments and the updated Zoning Map, PlaceWorks will draft an 
Implementation Plan to assist the public and City staff in understanding the key changes to format, style, and 
procedures in the updated Title 27. The implementation plan will be a concise document that uses graphics and other 
tools as applicable to make it user friendly. The plan could include some handouts or brochures to illustrate key 
sections of the zoning code update. This scope of work assumes permit checklist and applications will not be part of 
the Implementation Plan. 
After receiving City staff comments on the Draft Implementation Plan, PlaceWorks will prepare a Final 
Implementation Plan.  
As an optional task, PlaceWorks can build on the high-level evaluation of other plans in Task 2.4 to prepare a more 
detailed, line-by-line review of individual plan provisions and recommend detailed plan amendments for General Plan 
consistency. This scope assumes that any amendments to these plans would happen through a separate process. 
PlaceWorks will attend a training course with City staff to present the Final Implementation Plan and answer 
questions from staff on the updates to Title 27. It is anticipated the staff attending the training will be those who 
work at the counter and use the Zoning Code most frequently. 
Deliverables: 
»
Draft Implementation Plan (electronic PDF format)
»
Final Implementation Plan (electronic PDF format)
»
Optional: detailed evaluation of some or all other plans and recommended amendments
»
PowerPoint presentation and agenda for staff training
PlaceWorks’ proposed schedule for completion of the San Mateo Comprehensive Zoning Code Update is shown in 
Figure 1. As shown in the schedule, we anticipate that the comprehensive zoning code update can be completed by 
March 2027, with the implementation tasks completed by June 2027.  
PlaceWorks has a strong track record in meeting project schedules and coordinating closely with its clients. Over 
years of managing projects similar to the San Mateo Comprehensive Zoning Code Update, we have developed a 
variety of tools to keep projects on schedule and ensure that staff are well informed at all times: 
• We maintain an up-to-date schedule throughout the project, to ensure that all team members are aware of upcoming
meetings and product due dates.
• We stay in close, regular contact with staff and our subconsultants and document important decisions about the
project in writing, which ensures that decisions are understood by all team members.
• We schedule project due dates for staff and subconsultants with adequate time for editing and formatting into
finished reports.
198 of 1111

--- page 199 ---
• We limit subconsultants’ payments to specific milestones to ensure that progress on the project is commensurate
with billings.
Task 7.3, amendments to other Specific Plans, is an optional task. Costs for these tasks are not included in the costs in 
Table 2 as it is not possible to provide an accurate cost estimate until the potential amendments are identified.

Jennifer Rosas

Subject: San Mateo Comprehensive Zoning Code Update 
Dear Jennifer: 
Please accept the attached submittal as PlaceWorks’ proposal to prepare the Comprehensive Zoning Code 
Update for the City of San Mateo. 
Over the course of the Strive San Mateo project, which began in 2018, PlaceWorks got to know San Mateo 
residents, stakeholders, decision makers, and staff. We understand the nuances of working in San Mateo, 
including the overlapping layers of past planning documents; a passionate and informed public; and 
commitment to sustainability, environmental justice, and community engagement. We are not only 
intimately familiar with the big ideas of General Plan 2040, we share them as personal and professional 
values, so it is particularly meaningful to have the opportunity to move those ideas closer to reality through 
this zoning code update. However, we realize that the work ahead is not idealistic and aspirational, but 
technical, detailed, and complex. Moreover, it will be completed with the high level of public scrutiny 
typical of planning in San Mateo. Our knowledge of San Mateo is complemented by our experience 
bringing outdated zoning codes into the twenty-first century, making them more legible, clear, and 
intuitive. For this project, Carey Stone, Alexis Mena, and I will bring our familiarity with San Mateo and with 
General Plan 2040 and its environmental impact report (EIR), and we will add Amy Sinsheimer as a zoning 
expert and highly skilled project manager, creating an ideal team to meet the City’s needs.  
PlaceWorks has signed multiple contracts with the City of San Mateo. We are confident in our ability to 
arrive at Professional Services Agreement wording we can sign. If the City will allow it, we would appreciate 
the opportunity to submit proposed wording changes for consideration.  
Please do not hesitate to contact me if you need more information. The PlaceWorks team looks forward to 
working with you on this interesting and challenging project. 
We look forward to your response. 
Respectfully submitted, 
PLACEWORKS 
 
Joanna Jansen, AICP, LEED AP 
Managing Principal, Environmental Services

Proposal for Services  •  PLACEWORKS 
1 
1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
In March 2024, the City of San Mateo (City) adopted General Plan 
2040. To help address the region’s housing crisis, General Plan 2040 
increased San Mateo’s housing capacity by over 19,000 new homes 
and added space for 15,000 new jobs in areas close to transit and 
services, alongside many other ambitious policies and programs. The 
development community has responded to the vision of the new 
General Plan with significant interest; the City has received multiple 
applications for residential and mixed-use projects under Senate Bill 
(SB) 330 in 2024 and 2025. However, even for projects that are 
consistent with the land use designations and high-level policies of 
General Plan 2040, processing these applications is challenging for 
both staff and applicants as long as the zoning code remains 
inconsistent. Many applications include numerous waivers and 
exceptions to try to make a project consistent with both General Plan 
2040 and the zoning code.  
The provisions of General Plan 2040 to increase residential and 
mixed-use development in the 10 Study Areas, including the 
Downtown, Hayward Park, and Hillsdale stations, are broadly 
supported by City decision makers and housing advocates. Staff are 
eager to update the zoning code to simplify and clarify it. However, 
the complexity and expectations that have accumulated over decades 
as the current code has been amended, tweaked, and patched up 
piecemeal will require careful and deliberate work to undo. 
PlaceWorks has prepared a proposal to balance the City’s desire to 
move swiftly to implement General Plan 2040 with the need to 
include staff, stakeholders, and decision makers in a thoughtful and 
thorough process.  
To strike this balance, and in response to the Request for Proposals 
(RFP), this scope would accelerate work on some of the most pressing 
project components, namely updating the list of zoning districts and 
identifying the needed updates to the zoning map for consistency 
with the General Plan 2040 land use map and land use designations. 
We propose to have these two major components adopted 17 
months from project initiation. This scope also recommends an 
addendum to the General Plan 2040 EIR as the appropriate 
environmental document to facilitate adoption.

2. FIRM PROFILE AND CONTACT 
INFORMATION 
PlaceWorks is a 100 percent employee-owned planning, design, and environmental consulting firm, with 
approximately 140 employees in six offices. Serving both public- and private-sector clients throughout the state, 
PlaceWorks provides comprehensive planning, environmental review, urban design, landscape architecture, 
community outreach, and Geographic Information Systems (GIS) services. PlaceWorks was founded in 1975 as The 
Center for Planning and Research. The present-day firm resulted from a 2011 merger of The Planning Center, based in 
southern California, with Design, Community, & Environment (DC&E) in the Bay Area. 
PlaceWorks is financially stable and has sufficient capacity, staff, and resources to successfully complete the project 
on time and on budget. PlaceWorks has never filed bankruptcy and is in good financial standing.   
PlaceWorks does not have any bankruptcies, liens, stop-payment notices, judgments, lawsuits, arbitrations, 
mediations, foreclosures, or similar actions pending. In March 2021, PlaceWorks was named in a cross-complaint, and 
a settlement was reached and the lawsuit was dismissed with no admission of fault on our part in March 2024. The 
original complaint was filed in 2019 against the City of Oakland for breach of contract by Flatiron West Inc., the 
general contractor for the Embarcadero Bridge Replacement project. The City of Oakland cross-complained against 
T.Y. Lin International Group and AECOM in July 2020. T.Y. Lin/AECOM subsequently filed a cross-complaint against 
DC&E/PlaceWorks. DC&E was acquired by PlaceWorks in 2011, and our preconstruction planning role on the project 
ended in 2007.  
3. QUALIFICATIONS 
Relevant Projects 
SAN MATEO GENERAL PLAN UPDATE AND EIR (SAN MATEO, CA) 
PlaceWorks led a multidisciplinary team to update the City’s General Plan and 
prepare an EIR. General Plan 2040 was adopted in March 2024 and identifies 
solutions to address the critical need for housing, improving transportation 
connections citywide for all modes of travel, and confronting the effects of climate change and sea-level rise. Joanna 
Jansen, as the Principal-in-Charge, and Carey Stone, as Project Manager, led all phases of the project: establishing the 
vision and values for the future; creating and evaluating a range of land use and circulation alternatives; updating the 
goals and policies; and creating a user-friendly General Plan and associated EIR. The City emphasized equitable 
outreach throughout the process and PlaceWorks worked closely with the Peninsula Conflict Resolution Center to 
reach Spanish-speaking and low-income residents. The zoning code update is a critical next step in implementing 
General Plan 2040 and moving the community towards the future it envisions.  
TITLE 17 ZONING CODE COMPREHENSIVE UPDATE AND ACCESSORY DWELLING UNIT 
ORDINANCE (TUOLUMNE COUNTY, CA) 
Following adoption of the Tuolumne County General Plan in 2019, PlaceWorks 
assisted the County with a comprehensive zoning code update. The update 
focused on changes needed to implement the land use policy direction from the 
General Plan, including focusing growth and development into established 
communities, streamlining housing development, and promoting economic development in the county. The update 
also responded to new State law requirements, including recent legislation regarding housing production, accessory

dwelling units, and other new residential requirements. Through the zoning code update, PlaceWorks modernized the 
code and improved its user-friendliness and established a more logical and streamlined development review process. 
PlaceWorks worked closely with multiple committees, the Planning Commission, and Board of Supervisors to ensure 
the process aligned with County and Board priorities. 
PITTSBURG ZONING CODE UPDATE (PITTSBURG, CA) 
PlaceWorks planning and design staff, with Amy Sinsheimer as Project Manager, 
have been collaborating on a comprehensive code update following on urban 
design efforts to enhance the built environment from block to citywide scales. 
Attention to the importance of form in the key area of Bliss Avenue anticipated to transform the area around the 
Pittsburg e-BART station, plus the precise-plan level effort for transforming Century Plaza, has helped inform the 
comprehensive zoning code update now underway. The comprehensive code update is addressing staff, community, 
and decision maker key issues related to zoning. The project has included the convening of a technical advisory 
committee made up of City staff, focus group meetings with key community stakeholders, meetings with the Planning 
Commission and City Council, and public workshops. At all of these levels, the focus on translating community values 
into feasible development standards that lead to improved sense of place has been paramount. The comprehensive 
code update is expected to be completed in 2026. 
WILDOMAR GENERAL PLAN ZONING CONSISTENCY PROGRAM (WILDOMAR, CA) 
PlaceWorks prepared a comprehensive development code update to match the 
new General Plan for the city. As one of the newest cities in California, Wildomar 
adopted the Riverside County zoning code at the time of incorporation. With the 
update of a new General Plan, the City took the opportunity to create a bespoke 
development code that reflected the needs and vision of the city. The development code simplified the allowable 
uses, streamlined review and approval procedures, and referenced objective design standards previously adopted 
separately by the City. PlaceWorks prepared the development code edits in concert and coordination with the 
General Plan update, and both the General Plan and the development code were adopted at the same council 
hearing in December 2024. 
SOLEDAD SB 2 ZONING ORDINANCE UPDATE (SOLEDAD, CA) 
The PlaceWorks Housing Team, directed by Jennifer Gastelum and managed by Amy 
Sinsheimer, assisted the City of Soledad with implementing their 2020-2028 Housing 
Element, by updating their zoning ordinance and creating an Accessory Dwelling Unit 
(ADU) handbook. The work was funded by an SB 2 Planning Grant. The team’s work on 
the update included updating the zoning code in a variety of areas to be consistent with State housing legislation. The 
team facilitated the adoption of the zoning amendments through the city’s public hearing process and prepared the 
applicable CEQA analysis pertaining to the required amendments.  
GENERAL PLAN AND ZONING UPDATE TO IMPLEMENT THE 6TH CYCLE HOUSING ELEMENT 
(CUPERTINO, CA) 
PlaceWorks completed map and text amendments to the Cupertino Zoning 
Ordinance necessary to implement the City’s recently adopted 2023-2031 Housing 
Element. Key elements of the Zoning Ordinance update included a significant 
increase of allowable housing densities and incentives for the production of 
affordable housing through streamlined processes and regulatory standards that will lead to successful projects. As 
part of the project, the PlaceWorks team led a series of in-person and online focus group and public events and 
presentations to the Planning Commission and City Council to prepare the community for the changes in the built

INFILL PROGRAM AND OBJECTIVE DESIGN STANDARDS (SACRAMENTO COUNTY, CA) 
PlaceWorks assisted the County in updating its existing Infill Program to further 
encourage infill development and increase and accelerate the production of housing, 
especially affordable housing throughout the unincorporated county. As part of this 
work, PlaceWorks inventoried and prioritized potential areas for infill, analyzed 
development standards and regulatory impediments to development, and developed a toolkit of incentives and 
strategies to maximize infill opportunities. Findings and final recommendations for the Infill Program Update were 
informed by community and stakeholder outreach, including with County departments, partner agencies, and 
advocates. PlaceWorks was also asked to assist the County by updating its mixed-use development objective design 
standards as an initial implementation measure of the Infill Program Update. 
CONTRA COSTA COUNTY ZONING CODE UPDATE (CONTRA COSTA, CA) 
Contra Costa County, in the San Francisco Bay area, is a large and diverse county, 
encompassing farms and orchards, intact historic communities, high-density transit-
oriented neighborhoods, suburban subdivisions, and extensive natural open space. 
Guiding development and conservation across this diverse landscape is challenging 
given that major components of the County’s Zoning Code have been in place since the 1940s. PlaceWorks, with 
Joanna Jansen as Principal-in-Charge, is leading a zoning code update to significantly streamline the code and improve 
its accessibility and usability. Our work to update the County’s zoning code began with the preparation of a Zoning 
Update Report to create a roadmap for the comprehensive update. The comprehensive code update is now 
implementing this roadmap to modernize the code, improve accessibility, and implement the new General Plan 
adopted in November 2024.

5. HISTORY AND EXPERIENCE 
As demonstrated in Chapter 3, PlaceWorks has extensive experience with similar comprehensive zoning code updates 
and a well-established relationship with the City of San Mateo.

Zoning  
PlaceWorks’ staff bring decades of work experience supporting local agencies with zoning expertise and housing 
policy. We have prepared several zoning code updates for jurisdictions throughout California, representing a variety 
of different communities. Our zoning codes are highly customized to each individual community, and we work closely 
with each client to identify the approach that will most effectively achieve the community’s objectives. PlaceWorks 
has significant experience considering existing historic resources and districts in the zoning process. Our recent work 
spans from ensuring the preservation of targeted components of the San Jose Civic Auditorium, to creating a Transfer 
of Development Rights (TDR) program to protect historic buildings from demolition as part of the Downtown Oakland 
Specific Plan Rezoning project.  
At PlaceWorks, our approach to urban design is enhanced by an expert understanding of transit-oriented 
development, downtown revitalization, and corridor planning. We integrate cohesive transit and streetscape 
networks with their surrounding neighborhoods, buildings, open spaces, and public facilities—all while maintaining 
the unique character of each community. As experts in neighborhood design and site development, PlaceWorks’ 
interdisciplinary approach effectively integrates site engineering, land forming, project programming, community 
structure, building design, and landscape architecture. We provide our public- and private-sector clients with plans 
and designs that work. 
Local Experience 
PlaceWorks has a strong working knowledge and familiarity with the City of San Mateo. Recently, the PlaceWorks 
team led the City’s General Plan Update and EIR, which were adopted in March 2024. While simultaneously updating 
the General Plan, we also led the City’s Climate Action Plan Update, which was adopted in 2020, as well as reviewing 
the City’s Objective Design Standards. We have also provided environmental review and comprehensive planning 
services for the City throughout the years, including preparing the Hillsdale Station Area Plan and preparing an EIR for 
the Hillsdale Shopping Center North Block Reconfiguration Project.  
6. WORK PLAN 
This chapter describes the scope of services to be completed by the PlaceWorks team for the San Mateo Zoning Code 
Update. To facilitate your review of this proposal, we have prepared a concise scope that emphasizes key 
components of our approach to this project.  
We are flexible regarding the proposed scope of work and will work with you to prepare a more detailed scope when 
we enter into a contract. We also recognize that it may be necessary to alter the scope as the project progresses and 
would be happy to work with you to ensure the successful completion of the project.  
Scope of Work 
Task 1. Project Administration and Management 
1.1 
Project Kick-off Meeting 
PlaceWorks will meet with City staff to initiate the project via a virtual kick-off meeting. We will review the scope and 
schedule with a focus on the intersection of outreach events and project milestones, keeping in mind the goal of 
phasing the project. This meeting will establish project management procedures, including invoicing terms and 
communication protocols, and data collection approach. In advance of this meeting, the PlaceWorks team will 
prepare a data request document.  
212 of 1111

--- page 213 ---
Proposal for Services  •  PLACEWORKS 
7 
1.2 
Weekly Meetings with City Staff  
The PlaceWorks project management team will participate in weekly virtual status update meetings with City staff to 
ensure that the project remains on task and budget. Additional PlaceWorks staff will attend meetings as needed. It is 
assumed that the weekly status meetings will be conducted virtually and will average one hour each. PlaceWorks will 
prepare and share a meeting agenda and meeting materials prior to each meeting. PlaceWorks will also participate in 
periodic phone calls with the City’s Project Manager (upon the initiation of either party) on an as-needed basis to 
address any issues that arise between project team meetings.  
1.3 
Key Progress Meetings with City Staff 
The PlaceWorks team will join City staff for virtual progress meetings at important project milestones such as 
completion of certain research and analysis, key community engagement activities, following Planning Commission 
and Council study sessions, and ahead of Planning Commission and City Council adoption hearings. PlaceWorks will 
prepare and share a meeting agenda prior to each meeting. 
1.4 
Project Website 
PlaceWorks understands that the Strive San Mateo webpage used by the City for the General Plan Update will also be 
used for the Zoning Code Update. PlaceWorks is currently managing the Strive website and will continue to do so to 
provide information about the Zoning Code Update. We will post documents as soon as they are available to the 
public, including summaries of community input, which will be important to keep the community’s trust. The website 
will continue to be usable and functional on all interfaces, including mobile phones, tablets, and computers, and will 
include references/links to City social media accounts.  
1.5 
Ongoing Project Management 
PlaceWorks will conduct ongoing project management, including internal coordination, review of project budget, and 
preparation of monthly invoices and progress reports. Amy Sinsheimer will serve as the day-to-day contact for overall 
project management and will oversee coordination of the regular project meetings, budget, and schedule. 
Deliverables: 
» 
Detailed work program (schedule and timeline) 
» 
Detailed budget tracking document 
» 
Agendas and notes for kickoff meeting, weekly check-in calls, and key progress meetings 
» 
Monthly invoices and progress reports 
» 
Website mock-up and live versions, plus regular updates and linked documents 
Task 2. Zoning Code Assessment – Research, Analysis, and Phasing Plan 
2.1 
Best Practice Research 
PlaceWorks will work closely with staff to continue to collect Zoning Code concerns or issues that should be 
addressed as part of the comprehensive update. Following the assessment of background data, PlaceWorks will 
conduct a review of best practices to help guide subsequent Zoning Code update tasks. PlaceWorks will also work 
with City staff to identify potential “best practices” jurisdictions to respond to those issues, in addition to PlaceWorks 
existing database of best practices. This task will also include “deep dives” into six to eight particularly complex or 
challenging topics, such as building height, shared and unbundled parking, use classifications for current and future 
uses, or bird-safe regulations.  
2.2 
Map Consistency Analysis 
PlaceWorks’ GIS team will develop an internal webmap tool to overlay and compare General Plan Land Uses with 
existing zoning designations at the parcel level. Using the webmap, PlaceWorks will work with City staff to identify 
213 of 1111

--- page 214 ---
8 
CITY OF SAN MATEO  •  Comprehensive Zoning Code Update 
inconsistent parcels to be updated through the subsequent Zoning Map amendments in Phase 1 of the Zoning Code 
Amendments (Task 5.1). It is our understanding that this analysis is expected to identify thousands of parcels in San 
Mateo where the current zoning district no longer matches the new General Plan designations. PlaceWorks will work 
with City staff to discuss any of the parcels identified in the webmap, before City staff responds with 
recommendations of how to resolve inconstancies on each of the parcels. While this analysis will identify 
inconsistencies, we recognize that rezoning can be a sensitive issue for residents and property owners and we 
anticipate close collaboration with staff to determine the smoothest process for notifying residents and explaining 
these changes.  
2.3 
Text Consistency Analysis/Comparison Table 
PlaceWorks will prepare a table of comparisons, outlining the necessary Zoning Code updates needed to meet 
Transit-Oriented Communities (TOC) Policy requirements for density, housing affordability (if any), parking, station 
access, and circulation. This scope assumes that Metropolitan Transportation Commission’s (MTC’s) TOC policy will be 
substantially similar to the version in place as of April 2025. To remain consistent with General Plan 2040 and cover 
the Zoning Code updates with an addendum as described in Task 4, this scope also assumes that any identified policy 
changes for MTC TOC consistency that might trigger a General Plan Amendment would be addressed through a 
separate process.  
2.4 
Consistency Analysis of Other City Plans 
PlaceWorks will review and assess other adopted plans and policies against the adopted Strive 2040 General Plan. The 
adopted plans and policies the PlaceWorks team will review include: 
• Bay Meadows Specific Plan and Amendments (Phases 
I and II) 
• Detroit Drive Specific Plan 
• El Camino Real Master Plan 
• Hillsdale Station Area Plan 
• Mariner’s Island Specific Plan 
• Multifamily and Residential Mixed-Use Objective 
Design Standards 
• San Mateo Rail Corridor Transit-Oriented 
Development Plan 
• Shoreline Specific Plan 
• City Charter 
For each plan, PlaceWorks will prepare a high-level review of the major plan components, examples of plan provisions 
that conflict with General Plan 2040, and big-picture recommendations for addressing inconsistencies, whether through 
amendments to the other plan or policy, and/or retiring the other plan or policy, if appropriate. The process to complete 
amendments is addressed in Task 7.3.     
2.5 
Draft Framework Outline 
Based on input received through the previous tasks, PlaceWorks will prepare a draft framework outline that identifies 
the structure of the code, revisions to the existing code and new provisions to be added. Sections anticipated to be 
focused on in the framework outline include development standards, inclusionary housing requirements, and off-street 
parking regulations. The overall approach to identifying where change is needed will be guided by better addressing 
equity, affordable housing, mobility, community assets, infrastructure, resilience and climate adaptation. We will also 
prepare a template to illustrate the intended format and style of the zoning code. As part of this task, we will ensure 
that the code is set up to be easily accessible, interactive, and searchable. After staff review, PlaceWorks will discuss 
with staff any needed changes. 
2.6 
Project Phasing Plan 
PlaceWorks will draft a Phasing Plan to submit to City staff for review. The plan will consider the desired project 
schedule along with the importance of moving certain amendments forward on an earlier schedule, and the need to 
214 of 1111

--- page 215 ---
Proposal for Services  •  PLACEWORKS 
9 
gain community support and buy in on the different components of the zoning code update. The following things will 
be considered during development of the Phasing Plan: 
• The Zoning Map amendments phasing should facilitate community and property owner awareness while also 
supporting development proposals that are ready to move forward with entitlement.  
• The establishment of zoning districts for land use designations that do not currently have an associated district should 
be prioritized.  
• The processing of zoning code amendments and Zoning Map amendments should be managed concurrently.  
Following City staff review of the Draft Phasing Plan, PlaceWorks will prepare a Final Phasing Plan based on City staff 
comments. 
Deliverables: 
» 
Draft and final framework outline and recommended approach documents (electronic). 
» 
Draft and Final Phasing Plan (electronic). 
Task 3. Community Outreach and Engagement  
In addition to the engagement activities described in this task, Planning Commission and City Council study sessions 
detailed under Task 5.9 will be important sources of input for the project. 
3.1 
Community Outreach and Engagement Plan 
As an initial phase of this task, PlaceWorks will use input from the kick-off meeting to develop a Public Engagement 
Plan for the project. The Public Engagement Plan will provide goals, methods of engagement, and an anticipated 
schedule for outreach components. Keeping in mind the City’s goal to complete this project expeditiously, preliminary 
recommendations for engagement efforts include: 
• Two in-person and two virtual community workshops or open house events 
• Up to 12 stakeholder group meetings  
• Focused online surveys to informed stakeholders and frequent code users 
Each of these components is described in more detail in the following subtasks and may be refined or revised 
following the kick-off meeting. 
3.2 
Community Engagement Materials and Online Surveys 
In support of the community engagement, PlaceWorks will prepare the following outreach materials: 
• Citywide Mailer. PlaceWorks will prepare a citywide mailer to inform stakeholders about the project, provide a link to 
the project website, and publish initial outreach dates as available. This scope of work assumes the City will be 
responsible for the mailer printing and postage. 
• Flyers. PlaceWorks will prepare up to four project flyers. 
• Online Surveys. PlaceWorks will prepare up to four online surveys to seek feedback from frequent zoning code users 
to get input on targeted issues the update will tackle. 
• Project Mailing List Emails. PlaceWorks will prepare emails to announce upcoming outreach opportunities and public 
meetings and other key project milestones.  
• Social Media Posts. PlaceWorks will prepare social media posts for the City to share to update the public about the 
project. 
All materials will be prepared in English. PlaceWorks will work with the City to determine if some or all of the 
outreach materials should be translated into Spanish. 
215 of 1111

--- page 216 ---
10 
CITY OF SAN MATEO  •  Comprehensive Zoning Code Update 
3.3 
Stakeholder Meetings 
PlaceWorks will work with City staff to identify appropriate stakeholders, such as developers, real estate brokers, 
architects, business owners, community groups, government agencies, and property owners, to participate in the 
zoning code update process. We propose up to 12 small-group meetings with invited participants to discuss topics 
such as existing constraints, project feasibility and marketability, potential opportunities, and to review the draft 
code. PlaceWorks will facilitate the stakeholder meetings and will provide support through the preparation of emailed 
invitation language, meeting agendas, and discussion questions for City review. PlaceWorks will coordinate with the 
City to determine if these meetings will be conducted in person or virtually. We assume that the City will be 
responsible for inviting stakeholders to the meetings and managing RSVPs. 
3.4 
Community Meetings 
PlaceWorks will facilitate two virtual and two in-person community meetings in the form of an open house or 
workshop that will be held at key points of the planning process. Potential engagement points include introducing the 
project and receiving community feedback on zoning concerns and asking for feedback on the Phase 1 and Phase 2 
Public Review Draft Zoning Code Amendments. For each community meeting, PlaceWorks will prepare draft and final 
materials, including a meeting agenda, sign-in sheet, presentation, open house boards and/or workshop activity, 
comment cards, and handouts as needed. We assume up to three PlaceWorks staff, including one bilingual staff 
member, for each community meeting with additional staffing and interpretation provided by the City. 
3.5 
Community Outreach and Engagement Summary 
PlaceWorks will prepare an engagement summary that recaps all engagement efforts throughout the project. The 
summary will also document key themes and issues that we heard from the community, identify how the project 
responded to community input, and include raw data from outreach events.  
Deliverables: 
» 
Draft and Final Public Engagement Plan 
» 
Citywide Mailer 
» 
Flyers (4) 
» 
Online Survey (4) 
» 
Project Emails  
» 
Social Media Posts 
» 
Community Outreach and Engagement Summary 
Task 4. Environmental Document (CEQA)  
PlaceWorks will prepare an addendum to the Strive San Mateo General Plan 2040 EIR, herein referred to as the 
General Plan EIR” to evaluate the zoning code update. This proposed approach to prepare an addendum to the 
certified General Plan EIR is based on the presumption that the proposed zoning code update will be within the scope 
of and consistent with the adopted General Plan and would therefore not create any new or worsened impacts than 
those identified in the General Plan EIR. PlaceWorks recommends an addendum, rather than a tiered mitigated 
negative declaration, because an addendum is a more legally defensible CEQA document. The addendum process 
does not involve a scoping or response to comments process. Additionally, because the zoning code update is 
presumed to be consistent with the adopted General Plan, technical analysis is not required. 
4.1 
Initiate CEQA Process – Phase 1 
PlaceWorks’ CEQA Lead, Alexis Mena, will participate in a virtual meeting with PlaceWorks’ zoning team and City staff 
to kick off the CEQA phase of the project. The meeting focus will be to familiarize the CEQA team with the contents of 
the zoning code update, and to discuss the next steps and timeline for preparing the addendum. 
216 of 1111

--- page 217 ---
Proposal for Services  •  PLACEWORKS 
11 
4.2 
Project Description – Phase 1 
Following the CEQA kick-off meeting, PlaceWorks will prepare a draft project description of the proposed zoning code 
update. PlaceWorks will provide a revised project description based on City comments as part of the Administrative 
Draft Addendum described in Task 4.3. 
4.3 
Administrative Draft Addendum – Phase 1 
PlaceWorks will prepare an addendum to the certified General Plan EIR. The addendum will include qualitative 
discussions for each of the CEQA-required topical areas, referencing the conclusions in the General Plan EIR, 
wherever possible, for the conclusions in the addendum. A contract modification would be required for any new 
modeling or major quantitative analysis. PlaceWorks will submit the Administrative Draft Addendum electronically (in 
Word) for City review and comment. 
4.4 
Screencheck Draft Addendum – Phase 1 
Using one set of consolidated comments from City staff on the Administrative Draft Addendum, PlaceWorks will 
prepare a Screencheck Draft Addendum.  
4.5 
Final Addendum – Phase 1 
Using one set of consolidated comments from the City on the Screencheck Draft Addendum, PlaceWorks will prepare 
the Final Addendum. This task assumes that comments received on the Screencheck Draft Addendum will be minimal 
and require minor textual edits or revision. This scope of work also assumes that the Final Addendum will be posted 
online for public review but that a response to comments process will not be required. Therefore, this scope of work 
does not include any responses to comments that the City may receive on the addendum. However, we are available 
to prepare responses to comments and addendum revisions, if requested, for an additional cost. 
4.6 
Notice of Determination – Phase 1 
Following the approval of the addendum and within five days of approval of the zoning code update, PlaceWorks will 
prepare a Notice of Determination (NOD) for submittal to the County Clerk. PlaceWorks will submit the NOD to the 
Governor’s Office of Land Use and Climate Innovation’s State Clearinghouse. City staff will submit the NOD to the 
County Clerk, pay all applicable filing fees at the time of posting, and post on the City’s website. The budget does not 
include payment of any filing fees.  
4.7 
Initiate CEQA Process – Phase 2 
PlaceWorks’ CEQA Lead, Alexis Mena, will participate in a virtual meeting with PlaceWorks’ zoning team and City staff 
to kick off the CEQA phase of the project. The meeting focus will be to familiarize the CEQA team with the contents of 
the zoning code update, and to discuss the next steps and timeline for preparing the addendum. 
4.8 
Project Description – Phase 2 
Following the CEQA kick-off meeting, PlaceWorks will prepare a draft project description of the proposed zoning code 
update. PlaceWorks will provide a revised project description based on City comments on the draft project 
description as part of the Administrative Draft Addendum described in Task 4.9. 
4.9 
Administrative Draft Addendum – Phase 2 
PlaceWorks will prepare an addendum to the certified General Plan EIR. The addendum will include qualitative 
discussions for each of the CEQA-required topical areas, referencing the conclusions in the General Plan EIR, 
wherever possible, for the conclusions in the addendum. A contract modification would be required for any new 
modeling or major quantitative analysis. PlaceWorks will submit the Administrative Draft Addendum electronically (in 
Word) for City review and comment.

Using one set of consolidated comments from the City on the Screencheck Draft Addendum, PlaceWorks will prepare 
the Final Addendum. This task assumes that comments received on the Screencheck Draft Addendum will be minimal

online for public review but that a response to comments process will not be required. Therefore, this scope of work 
does not include any responses to comments that the City may receive on the addendum. However, we are available 
to prepare responses to comments and addendum revisions, if requested, for an additional cost.

Use and Climate Innovation’s State Clearinghouse. City staff will submit the NOD to the County Clerk and pay all 
applicable filing fees at the time of posting and post the NOD on the City’s website. The budget does not include 
payment of any filing fees.

Task 5. Updates to Zoning Code, Zoning Map, and Related Municipal Codes 
PlaceWorks proposes two phases to complete the zoning code update, with the first phase addressing most critical 
pieces of the update that need to be codified earlier. Phase 2 will address the remaining amendments. This approach 
is intended to meet City goals of adopting some amendments earlier in the process while aiming to keep the overall 
schedule close to two years from initiation to adoption of the Phase 2 zoning amendments. In both phases, the zoning 
code updates will emphasize user-friendliness for both staff and applicants, adding illustrations, tables, and matrices 
to clarify and simplify requirements and processes.  
5.1 
Phase 1 Administrative Draft Zoning Code Amendments 
PlaceWorks will prepare the first phase of the Administrative Draft Zoning Code Amendments for internal review and 
comment by City staff. Phase 1 will include:  
• Updates to the zoning map for consistency with the Strive 2040 General Plan land use map and land use designations  
• Adding, consolidating, removing, or modifying zoning districts, including for alignment with the General Plan 
• Translating stories into building-height limits to align with the General Plan 
• Definitions that accompany sections updated for Phase 1 
Before preparing Phase 1 of the Administrative Draft, PlaceWorks will consult with City staff to determine the 
appropriate file format(s) and preferred tracking procedures to ensure ease of internal use and review. It is assumed 
that the City will provide a single, consolidated set of comments following the review. 
218 of 1111

--- page 219 ---
Proposal for Services  •  PLACEWORKS 
13 
5.2 
Phase 1 Public Review Draft Zoning Code Amendments 
Following receipt of consolidated City staff comments, PlaceWorks will prepare a Public Review Draft of the Phase 1 
Zoning Code Amendments that is suitable to be published on the City’s website.  
5.3 
Phase 1 Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments  
Following public review, PlaceWorks will work with City staff to determine edits to make in response to comments 
received on the Phase 1 public review draft zoning code amendments. PlaceWorks will then make revisions and 
provide the Phase 1 Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments to City staff be provided to the Planning Commission 
and City Council for consideration for adoption. The Phase 1 Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments will address any 
typos or errors and will incorporate a summary of edits and responses to comments on the Public Draft from staff, 
Planning Commission, City Council, and the public.  
5.4 
Phase 1 Final Zoning Code Amendments  
Following Planning Commission and City Council adoption hearings for the Phase 1 zoning code amendments (see 
Task 6), PlaceWorks will prepare the Phase 1 Final Zoning Code with all revisions in PDF or other preferred format. We 
assume that the City will be responsible for updating the zoning code website (e.g., Municode). 
5.5 
Phase 2 Administrative Draft Zoning Code Amendments 
PlaceWorks will prepare the second and final phase of the Administrative Draft Zoning Code Amendments for internal 
review and comment by City staff. Determination of amendments to be included in the Phase 2 amendments will also 
occur during earlier tasks in this scope of work and will be detailed in the Phasing Plan in Task 2.6. Phase 2 
amendments will include all remaining amendments not addressed during Phase 1 and will be detailed in the Phasing 
Plan in Task 2.6. They will include, but are not limited to: 
• Possibly folding overlay district requirements into base zoning districts 
• Possibly removing the Bay Meadows Specific Plan zoning district  
• Revising development standards for each zoning district 
» 
Revising development standards in special areas such as Hillsdale Mall, Fashion Island, 25th Avenue, and El 
Camino Real 
» 
Updating floor-area ratio (FAR) for the C1 and C2 districts 
» 
Updating and consolidating FAR for all zoning districts 
» 
Updating permitted uses in each zoning district 
• Updating parking standards  
• Establishing a bird-safe building ordinance 
• Establishing a dark sky ordinance 
• Updating noise standards 
PlaceWorks understands the City is working on other zoning code amendments to be adopted separate from the 
comprehensive zoning code amendments, including, but not limited to, accessory dwelling unit and SB 9 updates, 
historic resources, certain parking requirements, and affordable housing requirements. PlaceWorks expects to 
include those adopted ordinances in the comprehensive zoning code update after they are adopted using the same 
organization as the comprehensive update template and will update cross-referencing as needed at that time.  
5.6 
Phase 2 Public Review Draft Zoning Code Amendments 
Following receipt of consolidated City staff comments, PlaceWorks will prepare a Public Review Draft of the Phase 2 
Zoning Code Amendments that is suitable to be published on the City’s website.

Phase 2 Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments  
Following public review, PlaceWorks will work with City staff to determine edits to make in response to comments

provide the Phase 2 Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments to City staff to provide to the Planning Commission and 
City Council for consideration for adoption. The Phase 2 Revised Draft Zoning Code Amendments will address any 
typos or errors and will incorporate a summary of edits and responses to comments on the Public Draft from staff, 
Planning Commission, City Council, and the public.

Phase 2 Final Zoning Code Amendments  
Following Planning Commission and City Council adoption hearings for the Phase 2 zoning code amendments (see 
Task 6), PlaceWorks will prepare the Phase 2 Final Zoning Code with all revisions in PDF or other preferred format. We 
assume that the City will be responsible for updating the Zoning Code website (e.g., Municode). 
5.9 
Planning Commission and City Council Study Sessions 
PlaceWorks will work with City staff to prepare for and attend in-person or virtual study sessions with the Planning 
Commission and City Council at key points in the project. This scope of work assumes up to six total in-person study 
sessions with either the Planning Commission or City Council with in-person attendance by at least one PlaceWorks 
team member. If virtual study sessions are determined to be preferable to have a greater number of meetings or 
attendance by more PlaceWorks staff, the budget under this task could likely accommodate a larger number of 
meetings. This scope of work assumes that meetings with approval bodies will correspond with project milestones, 
such as providing an overview of the project and phasing plan after the phasing plan is complete, to gather input on 
key issues being considered in best practices research and analysis, or at the time of the Phase 1 or Phase 2 Public 
Review Drafts.

team member. Additional hearings may be added to the scope and reflected in any necessary budget augments at the 
request of City staff.

Task 7. Implementation Plan 
7.1 
Draft Implementation Plan 
Following adoption of all Zoning Code Amendments and the updated Zoning Map, PlaceWorks will draft an 
Implementation Plan to assist the public and City staff in understanding the key changes to format, style, and 
procedures in the updated Title 27. The implementation plan will be a concise document that uses graphics and other 
tools as applicable to make it user friendly. The plan could include some handouts or brochures to illustrate key 
sections of the zoning code update. This scope of work assumes permit checklist and applications will not be part of 
the Implementation Plan. 
7.2 
Final Implementation Plan 
After receiving City staff comments on the Draft Implementation Plan, PlaceWorks will prepare a Final 
Implementation Plan.  
7.3 
Specific Plan Updates (optional task) 
As an optional task, PlaceWorks can build on the high-level evaluation of other plans in Task 2.4 to prepare a more 
detailed, line-by-line review of individual plan provisions and recommend detailed plan amendments for General Plan 
consistency. As noted in the Q and A, we assume that any amendments to these plans would happen through a 
separate process. 
7.4 
Staff Training 
PlaceWorks will attend a training course with City staff to present the Final Implementation Plan and answer 
questions from staff on the updates to Title 27. It is anticipated the staff attending the training will be those who 
work at the counter and use the Zoning Code most frequently. 
Deliverables: 
» 
Draft Implementation Plan (electronic PDF format) 
» 
Final Implementation Plan (electronic PDF format) 
» 
Optional: detailed evaluation of some or all other plans and recommended amendments 
» 
PowerPoint presentation and agenda for staff training

CITY OF SAN MATEO  •  Comprehensive Zoning Code Update 
Schedule 
PlaceWorks’ proposed schedule for completion of the San Mateo Comprehensive Zoning Code Update is shown in 
Figure 1. As shown in the schedule, we anticipate that the project can be completed by September 2027.  
PlaceWorks has a strong track record in meeting project schedules and coordinating closely with its clients. Over 
years of managing projects similar to the San Mateo Comprehensive Zoning Code Update, we have developed a 
variety of tools to keep projects on schedule and ensure that staff are well informed at all times: 
• We maintain an up-to-date schedule throughout the project, to ensure that all team members are aware of upcoming 
meetings and product due dates. 
• We stay in close, regular contact with staff and our subconsultants and document important decisions about the 
project in writing, which ensures that decisions are understood by all team members. 
• We schedule project due dates for staff and subconsultants with adequate time for editing and formatting into 
finished reports. 
• We limit subconsultants’ payments to specific milestones to ensure that progress on the project is commensurate 
with billings.

9. BUDGET 
As shown in Table 2, the estimated cost to complete the scope of work described in this proposal is $874,613.  
PlaceWorks recommends planning for a 5 to 10 percent contingency fund to cover any unforeseen out-of-scope work 
that might be necessary for the project. 
We are flexible regarding project costs and hope that you will not eliminate us from consideration based on cost 
alone. 
The billing rates for each team member are included in Table 2. 
PlaceWorks bills for its work on a time-and-materials basis with monthly invoices.
