POST PACKET 
 
 PUBLIC COMMENT 
 
227 of 1111

--- page 228 ---
From:
Jeremy Levine
To:
City Council (San Mateo)
Cc:
Zachary Dahl; Christina Horrisberger; Alex Khojikian
Subject:
Public Comment: Agenda Items 15 & 38, Comprehensive Zoning Update + Inclusionary Zoning Update
Date:
Monday, June 16, 2025 2:21:22 PM
Good afternoon city council members and staff, 
HLC appreciates the work staff have done to implement Measure T with a comprehensive
zoning update and implement the housing element with updates to the Below Market Rate
housing program. We hope both processes will result in a streamlined, modern zoning code
that facilitates development allowed by the voter-approved general plan update. Voters
supported the general plan update because they want more housing at all levels of
affordability, and the city should move as quickly as possible to make that housing easier to
build.  
To that end, we write with recommendations for changes and additions to Placework's
Proposal for Services and the Below Market Rate program update. 
Comprehensive Zoning Update
First, the city can reduce costs and potentially speed up the timeline by streamlining
community input. To the extent additional community input is necessary after the six-year
general plan update, it should focus on user input for the zoning code. Focus groups and
targeted surveys, as proposed in the Placeworks RFP response, may help generate useful
feedback. However, open houses will not yield new information after the many public
meetings that the city already held for the General Plan. Instead, the city should encourage
interested residents to attend planning commission and city council hearings. 
Bigger picture, in order to most effectively promote housing pursuant to the voter-approved
General Plan, both phases of the zoning code update should focus on broadly reducing the full
spectrum of constraints to housing. Ideally, this update will streamline the code and make it
more user friendly in a variety of ways, not just by modifying Floor Area Ratios or revising
development standards in special areas. Cities like Cambride, Massachussetes and Spokane,
Washington have recently moved to completely eliminate redundant policies like floor area
ratios, which are unnecessary in cities that already regulate height and density. 
San Mateo should strive to be similarly innovative. To that end, we recommend adding two
additional bullets to the phase 2 scope agreement: 
Streamline redundant density controls, including but not limited to Floor Area Ratios,
massing requirements, setbacks, minimum lot sizes, minimum unit sizes, and all other
development standards
Simplify the zoning code to remove circular references and broaden allowable uses in
all districts. 
Under today's code, developers in San Mateo regularly use a dozen or more density bonus
concessions or waivers to end run around the city's onerous rules. Property owners with less
experience simply forego development they might otherwise pursue due to the code's
complexity. Emphasizing code simplification will ensure San Mateo's zoning code update
results in a better development process--and more homes at all levels of affordability--rather
228 of 1111

--- page 229 ---
than inadvertently adding more hoops.
Adding In-Lieu Fees to the BMR Program

Updates to San Mateo's Below Market Rate housing program also present an opportunity to
make San Mateo's zoning code more accessible to a wider range of users. Specifically, HLC
supports efforts to promote alternative methods for compliance with inclusionary
requirements. The current staff report suggests allowing off-site development of affordable
units and adding "an option that allows developers to propose an alternative."

To maximize usability of the code, the city should also actively encourage an in-lieu fee
option for all projects (current city rules allow in-lieu fees for developments of six to ten units
but not more). In-lieu fees provide funding for deeply affordable homes, which on- and off-
site inclusionary development rarely provides (onsite inclusionary low-income homes
typically meet 80% AMI, whereas in-lieu fees can fund 100% affordable developments that
include some units at the 30-50% AMI level). The fees can result in more affordable homes in
the long term by facilitating more development overall, which results in more total fee revenue
to fund more homes.

Theoretically, the city's proposal to add "an option that allows developers to propose an
alternative" to meeting the BMR rules will provide flexibility to pay in-lieu fees. However,
that proposal fails to provide certainty, opening projects to discretionary approval.
Discretionary approval processes will add significant time to project approvals and require
substantial staff resources to review, decreasing the likelihood real projects will ever choose to
pay in-lieu fees. Clearly defined in-lieu fee options will guarantee future builders have a
pathway to develop that generates revenue for deeply affordable homes.

Both San Mateo's comprehensive zoning update and its Below Market Rate ordinance update
present an opportunity to promote flexibility in development. This flexibility will provide
more high-quality housing options at a range of affordability.

Thank you for your leadership and consideration,
Jeremy

Jeremy Levine (he « him)

Policy Manager

Housing Leadership Council of San Mateo County

June 16, 2025 
SUBJECT:
Public Use Easement Dedication Acceptance for Property Located at 200 N. Quebec Street – Adoption
RECOMMENDATION:
Adopt a Resolution to accept the grant of public use easement for pedestrian and vehicular parking access along Monte 
Diablo Avenue for the property located at 200 N. Quebec Street in substantially the form presented.
BACKGROUND:
Guy Fong (herein referred to as the “Applicant”) on November 11, 2023, submitted planning application PA-2023-075 with 
the Planning Division for a Special Use Permit for property located at 200 N Quebec Street to allow a community service 
use for a basketball training facility (collectively referred to as “Project”) in the City of San Mateo (“City”). The planning 
application was entitled under Resolution No. 2 (2025) by the Planning Commission at a regular meeting held on February 
25, 2025. 
The planning application approval was conditioned upon the dedication of an easement for pedestrian and vehicular 
parking access along Monte Diablo Avenue. The Project’s public improvements include installation of seven parking stalls 
and realigning the pedestrian sidewalk access. Four of the seven parking stalls will be dedicated for public use, and the 
remaining three are reserved for private use to satisfy the Project’s parking requirements. A portion of the proposed 
parking stalls extends into the Project’s property from the Public Right-of-Way, and the existing sidewalk will be relocated 
onsite. The total easement to be dedicated contains approximately 1,145 square feet by extending 11.66 ft into the Project 
property from the Public Right-of-Way along the Monte Diablo Avenue frontage.
BUDGET IMPACT:
All staff costs associated with review and approval of this easement dedication is paid for by the Applicant through permit 
fees based on the current Master Fee Schedule.
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
The acceptance of this easement dedication is not a project for CEQA because it can be seen with certainty that it will not 
cause a physical change in the environment (Public Resources Code 21065.)
NOTICE PROVIDED
All meeting noticing requirements were met.
ATTACHMENTS
Att 1 – Proposed Resolution

Alex Khojikian, City Manager

June 16, 2025 
SUBJECT:
Humboldt Street Paving with the City of Burlingame – Cost Sharing Agreement
RECOMMENDATION:
Approve a Cost Sharing Agreement with the City of Burlingame for the Paving of Humboldt Street in conjunction with 
Burlingame’s 2025 Street Resurfacing Project; and authorize the City Manager to execute the agreement in substantially 
the form presented. 
BACKGROUND:
Both the City of San Mateo and the City of Burlingame administer pavement management programs and construct annual 
pavement rehabilitation projects. The City of Burlingame has selected North Humboldt Street, between Peninsula Avenue 
and Amphlett Boulevard, as part of its 2025 Street Resurfacing Project. The city limit between the two cities lies within the 
center of the roadway in this portion of Humboldt Street. This joint effort aligns with both cities’ goals to leverage 
resources, promote regional cooperation, and improve infrastructure in a fiscally responsible manner.
 
This Cost Sharing Agreement will provide the City of Burlingame funds to include the City of San Mateo’s portion of 
Humboldt Street for pavement rehabilitation as part of their 2025 street resurfacing project. The agreement proposes that 
the City of Burlingame will serve as the lead agency for project delivery, including design, bidding, construction 
management, and inspection. The estimated fiscal obligation for the City of San Mateo is $150,000. This amount will be 
funded from the Citywide Street Rehabilitation Program budget.  
BUDGET IMPACT:
Sufficient funds are available in the Citywide Street Rehabilitation Program (469997) to fund this agreement. 
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
Approval of this Cost Sharing Agreement is categorically exempt from CEQA, because the underlying project within the City 
of San Mateo consists of the operation, repair, maintenance, permitting, leasing, licensing, or minor alteration of existing 
public or private structures, facilities, mechanical equipment, or topographical features, involving negligible or no 
expansion of existing or former use. (CEQA Guidelines Section 15301.)
NOTICE PROVIDED
All meeting noticing requirements were met.
ATTACHMENTS
Att 1 – Agreement

COST SHARING AGREEMENT FOR PAVING OF HUMBOLDT STREET IN 
CONJUNCTION WITH THE BURLINGAME 
2025 STREET RESURFACING PROJECT 
 
BETWEEN THE CITY OF BURLINGAME, 
AND  
THE CITY OF SAN MATEO 
 
 
This Cost Sharing Agreement for paving of Humboldt Street in conjunction with 
the Burlingame 2025 Street Resurfacing Project, Project No. 86970 (“Agreement”) entered 
into by and among the CITY OF BURLINGAME, a municipal corporation in the County 
of San Mateo, State of California, hereinafter called “Burlingame”, and the CITY OF SAN 
MATEO, a municipal corporation in the County of San Mateo, State of California, 
hereinafter called “San Mateo” (each a “Party” and collectively the “Parties”), and is made 
effective as of _________________, 2025 and is based on the following: 
 
R E C I T A L S: 
 
 
WHEREAS, the Parties desire to enter into an agreement to provide for the 
allocation of costs for construction and construction management of the application of a 
street resurfacing on Humboldt Street (between Rollins Road and Peninsula Avenue) in 
conjunction with the City of Burlingame’s 2025 Street Resurfacing Project, Project No. 
86970 (“Project”), as further described below, including staff costs, and to establish the 
roles and responsibilities of the parties for completion of construction and construction 
management, and to enter into this Agreement to establish those obligations; and, 
 
 
WHEREAS, certain elements incorporated into the Project may provide benefit to 
only certain parties to this Agreement and each Party agrees to pay the full costs of elements 
which are to their sole benefit and to share the cost of those elements that are of mutual 
benefit; and,  
 
241 of 1111

--- page 242 ---
Page 2 of 7 
 
City of Burlingame and City of San Mateo 
 
 
Cost Share Agreement –Humboldt Street Paving 
 
 
WHEREAS, the portions of the Project of interest to San Mateo are shown on 
Exhibit “A”, attached to and incorporated into this Agreement by this reference; and, 
 
 
WHEREAS, a detailed estimate of costs for construction, construction 
management, and design are shown on Exhibit “B”, attached to and incorporated into this 
Agreement by this reference. 
 
 
NOW, THEREFORE, IT IS HEREBY AGREED as follows: 
 
Section 1.  Definitions   
 
The following definitions apply to terms used in this Agreement: 
 
“Line-Specific Costs” means the actual cost of materials, labor and all associated 
incidentals to construct the improvements on Humboldt Street, including the billings of the 
contractor and any consultants. 
 
“Shared Costs” means the costs of labor and materials that are for the mutual benefit of the 
Parties including mobilization, storm water control, traffic control, and construction 
inspection. 
 
“Project Costs” includes all costs incurred by the Lead Agency specifically to implement 
and complete construction of the Project, including construction contractor costs and 
consultant construction inspection.  In addition, Project Costs include any liability the Lead 
Agency incurs in prosecuting the Project and any claims adjusting, claims investigation, 
and costs incurred in defending claims (including reasonable attorney fees) brought by any 
contractor or third party for damages arising out of the prosecution of the Project.   
 
“Lead Agency” means the agency responsible for performance of tasks required to 
implement and complete construction of the Project, or a portion thereof, on behalf of all 
242 of 1111

--- page 243 ---
Page 3 of 7 
 
City of Burlingame and City of San Mateo 
 
 
Cost Share Agreement –Humboldt Street Paving 
 
Parties, including tasks such as obtaining and overseeing construction contractors and 
professional services.  The City of Burlingame shall be the Lead Agency for the Project. 
 
Section 2.  City of Burlingame 2025 Street Resurfacing Project 
 
Burlingame performs an annual Street Resurfacing Project on various roadways in need of 
repair or treatment. Humboldt Street (between Rollins Road and Peninsula Avenue) is 
scheduled for resurfacing during the 2025 Street Resurfacing Project and has been selected 
for a mill and overlay treatment with pavement section repairs. This portion of Humboldt 
Street between Rollins Road and Peninsula Avenue is half within the jurisdictional 
boundary of Burlingame and half within San Mateo. In addition, striping improvements 
will be made in conformance with Caltrans Standard Specifications and Standard Details.  
 
The improvements are shown on Exhibit “A”, and estimated costs associated with this 
construction are shown on Exhibit “B”. 
 
Section 3.   Party Responsibilities. 
 
 
A.  
As Lead Agency, Burlingame will prepare bid documents, conduct the 
public bidding process, award the contract for the Project, and cause the Project to be 
constructed, all in compliance with applicable law.  Burlingame will be the primary contact 
with the contractor who is awarded the contract, subject to San Mateo’s right to initiate 
change orders or other reasonable directives with respect to San Mateo’s line-specific 
work.  To the furthest extent permitted by law, Burlingame shall issue a categorical 
exemption for the Project pursuant to Public Resources Code § 21000 et seq. and California 
Code of Regulations, Title 14, Sections 15000 et seq. (collectively, “CEQA”).  Burlingame 
shall include, in its contract with the contractor on the Project, a requirement that the 
contractor include San Mateo, its officials, officers, employees and agents as additional 
insureds and indemnify, defend, and hold harmless San Mateo, its officials, officers, 
employees and agents from any liability, claims, demands, suits or actions which may be 
made by any person resulting from the Project.

B. 
San Mateo shall review the design documents, bid documents and contract 
to be awarded.  Upon opening of the bids, San Mateo may decline to participate in the 
Project if costs exceed San Mateo’s expectations and may terminate this Agreement, with  
five business days’ written notice to Burlingame.  In the event of termination, San Mateo 
shall be responsible for its Line-Specific Costs which were incurred prior to termination.  
This Agreement is subject to San Mateo obtaining authorization from its City Council for 
the expenditure shown in Section 4(C) below.  San Mateo shall issue any categorical 
exemption or other CEQA notice which San Mateo may determine to be required for its 
line-specific work in addition to the categorical exemption noted in Section 3(A).  
 
Section 4. 
Financial Participation  
 
A. 
The Burlingame and San Mateo shall each be responsible for the independent 
funding of its portion of the Project Costs.   
 
B. 
Each Party shall pay its own Line-Specific Costs, which are estimated in Exhibit B.  
In addition, Burlingame and San Mateo shall pay Shared Costs as identified in this 
Agreement and on Exhibit B.  However, Burlingame and San Mateo understand and agree 
that this is an estimate and total amounts will be unknown until the Project is completed.  
It is understood that the total sum to be reimbursed to Burlingame by San Mateo shall be 
determined from the Project Costs reasonably incurred by Burlingame to construct those 
certain improvements on behalf of San Mateo that do not exceed the Expenditure Limit 
approved by San Mateo’s Council.  
 
C. 
The estimated fiscal obligation for work performed under this Agreement by 
Burlingame on behalf of San Mateo is $150,000. (“Expenditure Limit”).  If San Mateo’s 
share of the Project Costs exceed the Expenditure Limit due to unforeseen conditions, San 
Mateo may seek further authorization from its Council for funding.

B.

and without offset for any other financial obligations of the Parties.  
 
Section 6. 
Limitations 
 
This Agreement shall not affect the allocation of costs for treatment, capital improvements, 
or other services under any prior agreement.  The Parties shall continue to share costs for 
those services in accordance with the procedures set forth in prior agreements until they 
are amended or replaced. Except as modified herein, the terms of previous agreements shall 
continue in full force and effect.

employees, agents, consultants and contractors free and harmless from any and all claims,

or in equity, to property or persons, including wrongful death, in any manner arising out of

CITY OF SAN MATEO
CITY OF SAN MATEO
Agenda Report
City Hall
330 W. 20th Avenue
San Mateo CA 94403
www.cityofsanmateo.org
Agenda Number:  18
Section Name: CONSENT CALENDAR
Account Number: 82-4681
File ID: 25-9972
TO:
City Council
FROM:
Alex Khojikian, City Manager
PREPARED BY:
Public Works Department
MEETING DATE:
June 16, 2025 
SUBJECT:
Citywide Fire, Life, and Safety Inspection Testing and Maintenance Services — Agreement
RECOMMENDATION:
Adopt a Resolution to approve an alternative purchasing procedure to award a five-year agreement with APi National 
Service Group, Inc. for inspection, testing, and maintenance of Citywide fire, life, and safety systems in the amount of 
$591,375; approve a contingency reserve of $60,000; and authorize the Public Works Director to execute the agreement in 
substantially the form presented and issue change orders within the contingency amount. 
BACKGROUND:
In order to meet the latest Fire Safety Code requirements of the National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) standard 
adopted by the State of California, the City must have certified specialists perform regular inspections along with testing 
and maintenance of the wet fire sprinklers, fire pumps, and fire alarm systems at the 19 City facilities with systems that 
require these types of inspection and maintenance. 
San Mateo Municipal Code Section 3.60.070(h)(3) authorizes the City Council to approve alternative purchasing 
procedures when it finds doing so is in the best interest of the City. Sourcewell (formerly National Joint Powers Association 
or NJPA) is a government organization that solicits, receives, and reviews competitive bids for numerous activities, 
including fire, life, and safety system inspection, testing, and maintenance services. Sourcewell has conducted competitive, 
public bids to select APi National Service Group, Inc., a parent company of Western States Fire Protection Company, for 
five (5) years of inspection, testing, and maintenance of City-owned wet fire sprinklers, fire pumps, and fire alarm systems. 
The fifth year of the contract will include the five-year recertification testing. Western States Fire Protection Company will 
perform all the work as an affiliate of APi National Service Group. APi National Service Group, Inc. is affiliated with the 
State of California General Service Administration (GSA), as well as Sourcewell. Sourcewell and GSA obtain bids for fire 
suppression system inspection and certification services based on unit cost per square foot. APi National Service Group, 
Inc. offers the lowest cost per square foot under its contract with Sourcewell. Relying on competitive bidding through 
Sourcewell allows the City to complete required public improvements efficiently by eliminating the need to develop 
project specifications and soliciting bids through a project-specific competitive process.  
Based on the above, staff recommends adopting a resolution approving an alternative purchasing procedure to award an 
agreement to APi National Service Group, Inc. for inspection, testing, and maintenance of Citywide fire, life, and safety 
systems in the amount of $591,375. The agreement will consist of four (4) annual amounts of $112,411 and a fifth-year 
amount of $141,731. The fifth-year budget amount is greater than the other years in order to perform inspections and 
tests that were performed during 2024 and are only required every five years. A contingency of $60,000 is requested to 
address any potential additional inspection, testing, or maintenance needs that may arise through the term of the 
agreement.
251 of 1111

--- page 252 ---
CITY OF SAN MATEO
BUDGET IMPACT:
Funding for these services is budgeted annually in the Fleet and Building Maintenance Fund. Pending approval and 
adoption of the Fiscal Year 2025-26 operating budget, sufficient funding will be available to cover the cost of this 
agreement.
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
Approval of an agreement for inspection, testing, and maintenance of Citywide fire, life, and safety systems is exempt from 
CEQA review as an “existing facility,” because it consists of the operation, repair, maintenance, permitting, leasing, 
licensing, or minor alteration of existing public or private structures, facilities, mechanical equipment, or topographical 
features, involving negligible or no expansion of existing or former use. (CEQA Guidelines Section 15301.)
NOTICE PROVIDED
All meeting noticing requirements were met.

CITY OF SAN MATEO 
RESOLUTION NO. ____ (2025) 
 
APPROVING AN ALTERNATIVE PURCHASING PROCEDURE TO AWARD AN AGREEMENT FOR CITYWIDE FIRE, 
LIFE, AND SAFETY INSPECTION TESTING AND MAINTENANCE SERVICES TO API NATIONAL SERVICE GROUP, 
INC.
WHEREAS, the City must have certified specialists perform regular inspections along with testing and 
maintenance of the wet fire sprinklers, fire pumps, and fire alarm systems at City facilities In order to meet the 
latest Fire Safety Code requirements of the National Fire Protection Association (NFPA) standard adopted by the 
State of California; and
WHEREAS, the City owns 19 facilities with systems that require these types of inspection and 
maintenance; and
WHEREAS, San Mateo Municipal Code Section 3.60.070(h)(3) authorizes the City Council to
approve alternative purchasing procedures upon a finding that it is in the best interest of the City; and
WHEREAS, Sourcewell (formerly known as National Joint Powers Association or NJPA) conducted a 
competitive bidding process and received qualified bids from companies affiliated with the National Fire 
Sprinkler Association and the International Facility Management Association; and
WHEREAS, pursuant to this competitive bidding process, Sourcewell has selected APi National Service 
Group, Inc. on behalf of member agencies; and
WHEREAS, use of Sourcewell’s selected vendor allows the City to complete required inspections and 
maintenance efficiently by eliminating the need to develop specifications and eliminating solicitation of bids 
through a project specific competitive bidding process; and
WHEREAS, APi National Service Group, Inc. offers the lowest cost per square foot under its contract with 
Sourcewell (formerly known as NJPA).
  
NOW, THEREFORE, THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA, HEREBY FINDS AND 
RESOLVES that: 
1.
Approval of an agreement with APi National Service Group, Inc. for inspection, testing, and 
maintenance of Citywide fire, life, and safety systems is exempt from CEQA review as an “existing 
facility,” because it consists of the operation, repair, maintenance, permitting, leasing, licensing, or 
minor alteration of existing public or private structures, facilities, mechanical equipment, or 
topographical features, involving negligible or no expansion of existing or former use. (CEQA 
Guidelines Section 15301.)
2.
Pursuant to San Mateo Municipal Code Section 3.60.070(h)(3), approval of an agreement with APi 
National Service Group, Inc. using Sourcewell is in the best interest of the City because:
a.
Sourcewell is a government organization that solicits, receives, and reviews competitive bids 
for numerous activities for government and education entities;  
b.
Sourcewell has conducted competitive bidding to receive qualified bids from companies 
affiliated with the National Fire Sprinkler Association and the International Facility 
Management Association;
c.
Sourcewell has selected Api National Service Group, Inc. on behalf of member agencies;
253 of 1111

--- page 254 ---
d.
Using Sourcewell’s competitive bid process allows the City to complete to efficiently 
complete required inspections and maintenance efficiently by eliminating the need to 
develop specifications for solicitation while ensuring a competitive price; and
e.
Api National Service Group, Inc. offers the lowest cost per square foot under its Sourcewell 
managed contract.
3.
A five-year agreement with APi National Service Group, Inc. for Citywide Fire, Life, and Safety 
Inspection Testing and Maintenance Services in the amount of $591,375 is approved.
4.
The Public Works Director is authorized to execute the agreement in substantially the form 
presented.

and in the manner set forth herein.

Agreement.
CONTRACTOR shall be responsible for employing or engaging all persons necessary to perform 
the services of CONTRACTOR.

CONTRACTOR agrees to comply with the City’s minimum wage ordinance as codified in 
Municipal Code Chapter 5.92, which differs from the state minimum wage and includes a Consumer 
Price Index escalator. 
CONTRACTOR or subcontractor shall not be qualified to bid on, be listed in a bid proposal, or 
engage in the performance of any contract for public work unless currently registered and qualified to

SECTION 15 - WAIVERS
The waiver by either party of any breach or violation of any term, covenant, or condition of this 
Agreement or of any provisions of any ordinance or law shall not be deemed to be a waiver of such 
term, covenant, condition, ordinance or law or of any subsequent breach or violation of the same or of 
any other term, condition, ordinance, or law.  The subsequent acceptance by either party of any fee or 
other money which may become due hereunder shall not be deemed to be a waiver of any preceding 
breach or violation by the other party of any term, covenant, or condition of this Agreement or of any 
applicable law or ordinance.
SECTION 16 - COSTS AND ATTORNEY FEES
Attorney fees in total amount not exceeding $5000, shall be recoverable as costs (by the filing 
of a cost bill) by the prevailing party in any action or actions to enforce the provisions of this 
Agreement.  The above $5000 limit is the total of attorney fees recoverable whether in the trial court, 
appellate court, or otherwise, and regardless of the number of attorneys, trials, appeals, or actions.  It 
is the intent of this provision that neither party shall have to pay the other more than $5000 for 
attorney fees arising out of an action, or actions to enforce the provisions of this Agreement.
SECTION 17 - NON-DISCRIMINATION
CONTRACTOR warrants that it is an Equal Opportunity Employer and shall comply with 
applicable regulations governing equal employment opportunity.  Neither CONTRACTOR nor any of its 
subcontractors shall discriminate in the employment of any person because of race, color, national 
origin, ancestry, physical handicap, medical condition, marital status, sex, or age, unless based upon a 
bona fide occupational qualification pursuant to the California Fair Employment and Housing Act.
SECTION 18 - MEDIATION
Should any dispute arise out of this Agreement, any party may request that it be submitted to 
mediation.  The parties shall meet in mediation within 30 days of a request.  The mediator shall be 
agreed to by the mediating parties; in the absence of an agreement, the parties shall each submit one 
name from mediators listed by either the American Arbitration Association, the State Mediation and 
Conciliation Service, or other agreed-upon service.  The mediator shall be selected by a blind draw.
The cost of mediation shall be borne equally by the parties.  Neither party shall be deemed the 
prevailing party.  No party shall be permitted to file a legal action without first meeting in mediation 
and making a good faith attempt to reach a mediated settlement.  The mediation process, once 
commenced by a meeting with the mediator, shall last until agreement is reached by the parties but 
not more than 60 days, unless the maximum time is extended by the parties.

EXHIBIT C
INSURANCE REQUIREMENTS
MINIMUM SCOPE OF INSURANCE 
Coverage shall be at least as broad as:
1. Commercial General Liability (CGL):  Insurance Services Office (ISO) Form CG 00 01 12 07 
covering CGL on an “occurrence” basis, including products-completed operations, personal & 
advertising injury, with limits no less than $2,000,000 per occurrence. If a general aggregate limit 
applies, either the general aggregate limit shall apply separately to this project/location or the 
general aggregate limit shall be twice the required occurrence limit.
2. Automobile Liability:  ISO Form Number CA 00 01 covering any auto (Code 1), or if Contractor 
has no owned autos, hired, (Code 8) and non-owned autos (Code 9), with limit no less than 
$1,000,000 per accident for bodily injury and property damage.
3. Workers’ Compensation: as required by the State of California, with Statutory Limits, and 
Employer’s Liability Insurance with limit of no less than $1,000,000 per accident for bodily injury 
or disease. 
If the contractor maintains higher limits than the minimums shown above, the City requires and shall be 
entitled to coverage for the higher limits maintained by the contractor.
Other Insurance Provisions
The insurance policies are to contain, or be endorsed to contain, the following provisions:
Additional Insured Status
The City, its elected and appointed officials, employees, and agents are to be covered as insureds on 
the auto policy for liability arising out of automobiles owned, leased, hired or borrowed by or on behalf 
of the Contractor; and on the CGL policy with respect to liability arising out of work or operations 
performed by or on behalf of the Contractor including materials, parts or equipment furnished in 
connection with such work or operations. General liability coverage can be provided in the form of an 
endorsement to the Contractor’s insurance (at least as broad as ISO Form CG 20 10, 11 85 or both CG 
20 10 and CG 20 37 forms if later revisions used).
Primary Coverage
For any claims related to this contract, the Contractor’s insurance coverage shall be primary insurance 
as respects the City, its elected and appointed officials, employees, and agents. Any insurance or self-
insurance maintained by the City, its elected and appointed officials, employees, or agents shall be 
excess of the Contractor’s insurance and shall not contribute with it.
Notice of Cancellation
Each insurance policy required above shall provide that coverage shall not be canceled, except after 
thirty (30) days’ prior written notice (10 days for non-payment) has been given to the City.
Waiver of Subrogation
271 of 1111

Page 18 of 18
Rev. 9/22
Contractor hereby grants to City a waiver of any right to subrogation which any insurer of said Contractor 
may acquire against the City by virtue of the payment of any loss under such insurance.  Contractor 
agrees to obtain any endorsement that may be necessary to effect this waiver of subrogation, but this 
provision applies regardless of whether or not the City has received a waiver of subrogation 
endorsement from the insurer.  
Deductibles and Self-Insured Retentions
Any deductibles or self-insured retentions must be declared to and approved by the City. The City may 
require the Contractor to purchase coverage with a lower deductible or retention or provide proof of 
ability to pay losses and related investigations, claim administration, and defense expenses within the 
retention.  
Acceptability of Insurers
Insurance is to be placed with insurers with a current A.M. Best’s rating of no less than A:VII, unless 
otherwise acceptable to the City.
Verification of Coverage
Contractor shall furnish the City with original certificates and amendatory endorsements or copies of 
the applicable policy language effecting coverage required by this clause.  All certificates and 
endorsements are to be received and approved by the City before work commences.  However, failure 
to obtain the required documents prior to the work beginning shall not waive the Contractor’s 
obligation to provide them.  The City reserves the right to require complete, certified copies of all 
required insurance policies, including endorsements required by these specifications, at any time.

June 16, 2025 
SUBJECT:
Building Access Control System Maintenance – Agreement 
RECOMMENDATION:
Adopt a Resolution to approve an alternative purchasing procedure to award an agreement to Edgeworth Integration, LLC, 
DBA VAS Security Systems for Building Access Control System Maintenance in the amount of $120,000; establish a 
contingency in the amount of $12,000; and authorize the Public Works Director to execute the agreement in substantially 
the form presented and issue change orders within the contingency amount.
BACKGROUND:
In 2019, the City engaged Edgeworth Integration, LLC, DBA VAS Security Systems, Inc. (Edgeworth) to furnish and install an 
upgrade to the City-wide building access control system through a Request for Proposal (RFP) process which featured 
thirteen prospective firms.  
The City selected Avigilon as its access control system, a proprietary system that can only be provided and serviced by 
Edgeworth, who has been responsible for its maintenance since installation in 2020. In May 2025, the City requested a 
proposal from Edgeworth for general maintenance services over the next five years. This proposal includes hardware and 
software repairs, onsite and online technical support, and firmware upgrades. Based on comparable maintenance 
programs for similar equipment across the City, the proposed cost of $120,000 for the five-year period is considered 
reasonable and fair.
San Mateo Municipal Code Section 3.60.050(b) authorizes the City Council to approve an alternative purchasing procedure 
when calling for bids is impracticable, unavailing, or impossible. In this case, staff recommends awarding the building 
access control system maintenance agreement to Edgeworth because they are the only qualified vendor to perform these 
services on the specific equipment. 
BUDGET IMPACT:
Funding for building access control system maintenance is budgeted annually in the Facilities Maintenance operating 
budget. Pending approval of the Fiscal Year 2025-26 budget, sufficient funding will be available to cover the cost of this 
agreement.
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
This project is not a project subject to CEQA, because it can be seen with certainty that it will not cause a physical change 
in the environment. (Public Resources Code Section 21065.)
NOTICE PROVIDED

CITY OF SAN MATEO
RESOLUTION NO. ____ (2025)
APPROVING AN ALTERNATIVE PURCHASING PROCEDURE TO AWARD AN AGREEMENT FOR BUILDING ACCESS 
CONTROL SYSTEM MAINTENANCE WITH EDGEWORTH INTEGRATION, LLC DBA VAS SECURITY SYSTEMS IN THE 
AMOUNT OF $120,000
WHEREAS, the City of San Mateo currently operates a building access control system that is proprietary 
to Edgeworth Integration, LLC DBA Vas Security Systems (Edgeworth); and
WHEREAS, the City has contracted with Edgeworth for building access control system maintenance 
services since 2020 and the term of the current building access control system maintenance services agreement 
expires on June 30, 2025; and
WHEREAS, in May 2025, staff requested a proposal from Edgeworth; and
WHEREAS, San Mateo Municipal Code Section 3.60.030 generally requires competitive bidding for 
contacts of $100,000 or more; and
WHEREAS, San Mateo Municipal Code Section 3.60.050(b) authorizes the City Council to approve an 
alternative purchasing procedure where calling for bids is impracticable, unavailing, or impossible; and 
WHEREAS, based on these circumstances, staff recommends award of an agreement to Edgeworth in 
the amount of $120,000 for building access control system maintenance services.
NOW, THEREFORE, THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF SAN MATEO, CALIFORNIA, HEREBY RESOLVES 
that: 
1.
Adoption of this resolution approving an alternative purchasing procedure to award an agreement to 
Edgeworth Integration, LLC DBA Vas Security Systems is not a project subject to CEQA, because it can be 
seen with certainty that there is no possibility that building access control system maintenance services 
may have a significant effect on the environment. (CEQA Guidelines Section 15061(b)(3).) 
2.
In accordance with San Mateo Municipal Code Section 3.60.050(b), calling for bids is 
impracticable, unavailing, or impossible because the current control system equipment is 
proprietary and can only be provided and serviced by Edgeworth Integration, LLC DBA Vas 
Security Systems.
3.
The award of a custodial services agreement to Edgeworth Integration, LLC DBA Vas Security Systems in 
the amount of $120,000, with a contingency reserve in the amount of $12,000, is approved.
4.
The Public Works Director is authorized to execute the agreement in substantially the form 
presented and issue change orders within the contingency amount.

Agreement.  The above $5000 limit is the total of attorney fees recoverable whether in the trial 
court, appellate court, or otherwise, and regardless of the number of attorneys, trials, appeals, or 
actions.  It is the intent of this provision that neither party shall have to pay the other more than 
$5000 for attorney fees arising out of an action, or actions to enforce the provisions of this 
Agreement.

Housing Act.
SECTION 18 - MEDIATION
Should any dispute arise out of this Agreement, any party may request that it be submitted 
to mediation.  The parties shall meet in mediation within 30 days of a request.  The mediator shall 
be agreed to by the mediating parties; in the absence of an agreement, the parties shall each 
submit one name from mediators listed by either the American Arbitration Association, the State 
Mediation and Conciliation Service, or other agreed-upon service.  The mediator shall be selected 
by a blind draw.
The cost of mediation shall be borne equally by the parties.  Neither party shall be deemed 
the prevailing party.  No party shall be permitted to file a legal action without first meeting in 
mediation and making a good faith attempt to reach a mediated settlement.  The mediation 
process, once commenced by a meeting with the mediator, shall last until agreement is reached by 
the parties but not more than 60 days, unless the maximum time is extended by the parties.

EXHIBIT A
SCOPE OF SERVICES
Scope of Work for Access Control System Maintenance
1. Objective
The primary objective of this maintenance agreement is to ensure all access control 
systems installed throughout City of San Mateo facilities remain in optimal working 
condition. This includes routine inspections, prompt repairs, and adherence to all 
applicable codes and policies. The goal is to safeguard public and employee access, ensure 
physical security, and maintain operational continuity across all applicable facilities.
2. Buildings Covered
This agreement applies to all City-owned or City-managed buildings requiring access 
control systems. These include, but are not limited to:
•
City Hall
•
San Mateo Police Department Headquarters
•
Public Works Department Facilities and Warehouses
•
Community Centers (e.g., King Center, Martin Luther King Jr. Center)
o
Martin Luther King Jr. Center
o
Beresford Center
o
Lakeshore Center
o
Shoreview Center
•
City Libraries:
o
Main Library
o
Marina Branch
o
Hillsdale Branch
•
Satellite and Administrative Facilities
o
City-operated office annexes
o
Storage and vehicle maintenance yards
Note: The San Mateo Wastewater Facility is explicitly excluded from this scope.
3. System Components Covered
The scope of work includes maintenance, troubleshooting, and replacement services 
for all hardware and software components that form part of the access control 
infrastructure. This includes:
•
Access Devices
o
Card readers (proximity, contactless, or biometric where applicable)
o
Keypad entry systems (if integrated with access control)
•
Door Hardware & Controls
o
Electriﬁed strikes and magnetic locks
o
Door controllers
o
Emergency override and egress systems
o
Auto-closers (only if integrated into access systems)
•
Control Infrastructure
o
Access control panels
282 of 1111

--- page 283 ---
o
Power supplies and backup batteries
o
Interface modules and data cables
o
Network-based access servers and control software (if under 
maintenance contract)
4. Services Included
4.1 Preventive Maintenance (Quarterly)
The vendor will perform proactive inspections and testing on a quarterly basis, 
including but not limited to:
•
Comprehensive inspection of all installed access hardware
•
Testing of entry and exit mechanisms under normal and emergency conditions
•
Functionality testing of fail-safe and fail-secure mechanisms, particularly on doors 
serving high-traffic public areas (e.g., libraries, recreation centers)
•
Documentation of system status and any issues identiﬁed, especially at recreation 
facilities and centers that host children, seniors, or large community gatherings
•
Clean, calibrate, and adjust devices as needed to maintain performance and longevity
4.2 Corrective Maintenance (As-Needed / On-Call Basis)
•
Diagnose issues with malfunctioning equipment reported by City personnel or 
discovered during inspections
•
Notify designated contacts at Edgeworth Integration for quotation and repair 
scheduling
•
Provide labor and replacement quotes at reduced, pre-negotiated service rates
•
Repairs include, but are not limited to:
o
Damaged, non-responsive, or vandalized badge/card readers
o
Faulty electric door strikes or locking mechanisms
o
Access control panel errors, software lockouts, or server issues
o
Power supplies, wiring failures, or low-voltage component failures
5. Hours of Operation
•
Standard Maintenance Hours:
Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM (non-holiday business hours)
•
Emergency Support Availability:
24 hours a day, 7 days a week for access control failures impacting:
o
Public-facing facilities (e.g., Libraries, Community Centers)
o
Safety-critical operations (e.g., Police Department, recreation centers 
serving youth or seniors)
o
Emergency ingress/egress routes or ADA-compliant pathways
• Exclusions
The following are excluded from this scope of work unless otherwise agreed upon:
•
Maintenance of access systems at the San Mateo Wastewater Facility
•
Network infrastructure that is not directly related to the access control 
system (e.g., unrelated servers, routers)
283 of 1111

--- page 284 ---
•
Building management systems or HVAC automation systems not connected to or 
reliant on access control interface
•
Upgrades or expansions to access control systems unless included in a separate 
contract
Edgeworth Integration will provide the quoted equipment, installation, and/or services.
Four trips per year to test all access control equipment; check and replace all panel 
batteries as needed; check all doors to correct functions; check readers at all locations.

FROM:
Alex Khojikian, City Manager
PREPARED BY:
Public Works Department
MEETING DATE:
June 16, 2025 
SUBJECT:
Wastewater Treatment Plant Chemicals — Purchase Contracts Award
RECOMMENDATION:
Award purchase contracts of chemical supplies necessary for Wastewater Treatment Plant operations to the following 
chemical suppliers: 1) Univar Solutions USA LLC to supply sodium hypochlorite in the amount of $1,455,820.00; sodium 
bisulfite in the amount of $285,134.63; sodium hydroxide in the amount of $457,985.51; and citric acid in the amount of 
$55,689.50; 2) Pencco, Inc. to supply ferric chloride in the amount of $496,272.38;  and authorize the Public Works 
Director to execute the contracts in substantially the form presented. 
BACKGROUND:
The Wastewater Treatment Plant (WWTP) uses chemicals to treat wastewater in accordance with applicable regulatory 
requirements. The five chemicals addressed in this report are sodium hypochlorite, sodium bisulfite, sodium hydroxide, 
citric acid, and ferric chloride. The City is a member of the Bay Area Clean Water Agencies’ (BACWA) Bay Area Chemical 
Consortium (BACC), which conducts a competitive bidding process for its member agencies annually.
In past years, the BACC competitive bidding process resulted in several chemical suppliers being identified as the lowest 
responsive bidders. This year, the bidding process resulted in Univar Solutions USA LLC (Univar) as the lowest responsive 
bidder for four of the five chemicals, which include sodium hypochlorite, sodium bisulfite, sodium hydroxide, and citric 
acid.  For the fifth chemical, ferric chloride, Pennco, Inc. was determined to be the lowest responsive bidder. The 
paragraphs below describe the chemical uses, bidding results, and staff recommended contractor and contract amounts. 
The terms of all contracts are 12 months, and the prices are firm for the entire period.
Sodium Hypochlorite
The WWTP utilizes sodium hypochlorite as an oxidizing agent in the disinfection process. Disinfection is a requirement of 
the National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permit. This contract is to supply and deliver 400,000 gallons 
of this product. The BACC bid result for sodium hypochlorite is summarized below:

Sodium Bisulfite
The WWTP utilizes sodium bisulfite as a reducing agent in the dechlorination process prior to discharging effluent to the 
San Francisco Bay. The dechlorination process is a requirement of the NPDES permit. This contract is to supply and deliver 
180,000 gallons of this product. The BACC bid result for sodium bisulfite is summarized below:

Sodium Hydroxide
The WWTP utilizes sodium hydroxide to adjust the alkalinity of wastewater in biological nutrient removal and for pH

Ferric Chloride
The WWTP utilizes ferric chloride for the control of hydrogen sulfide, a flammable gas produced in the anaerobic digestion 
process. Managing the sulfur content of combusted digester gas is a requirement of the Bay Area Air District permit. This 
contract includes the supply and delivery of an estimated 300 dry tons of product. The BACC bid result for ferric chloride is 
summarized below:

BUDGET IMPACT:
289 of 1111

--- page 290 ---
CITY OF SAN MATEO
Funding for the supply of chemicals is budgeted annually in the WWTP operating budget. Due to existing fluctuations in 
chemical needs, which can be weather dependent, as well as the unknown impact on chemical usage with the addition of 
the new WWTP facility, the quantity of each chemical has been estimated on the high end of the total potential needs 
anticipated by the designers of the new facility. Staff will monitor chemical usage under these contracts and funding 
availability and return to Council as needed if additional funding is needed exceeding the available budget.  
ENVIRONMENTAL DETERMINATION:
The purchase of various chemicals to enable the treatment of wastewater at the City’s Wastewater Treatment Plant is not 
a project subject to CEQA, because it is a continuing maintenance activity involving the purchase of supplies. (CEQA 
Guidelines Section 15378(b)(2).)

SUPPLIER will be required to furnish and update, as necessary, a list of telephone

11.
Time of Delivery. SODIUM HYPOCHLORITE will be furnished F.O.B. Destination City 
of San Mateo Water Quality Control Plant 2050 Detroit Drive, San Mateo, CA (i.e., 
Seller/SUPPLIER pays freight charges, owns goods in transit and files any damage claims). 
Deliveries will be made in 3,000-gallon minimum and 5,000-gallon maximum loads.
If SUPPLIER is located outside the 650 area code, then it is requested to provide a toll-
f r e e  telephone number for placing orders and an email address to be used by the CITY to 
send confirmation of order.
Deliveries of SODIUM HYPOCHLORITE will generally be prescheduled to provide 
SUPPLIER with at least three (3) days’ notice before delivery must be made, but SUPPLIER 
must have the ability after notification to deliver the chemical within 24 hours.
Orders will be placed by phone and confirmed by email. SUPPLIER must email a 
confirmation of the CITY's order within twenty-four (24) hours, unless the CITY requires 
delivery within twenty-four (24) hours then confirmation must be made within three (3) hours. 
In the event SUPPLIER fails to provide the chemicals within twenty-four (24) hours or other 
scheduled delivery timeframe and the CITY is forced to obtain the chemical from an alternative 
source, all additional CITY cost incurred will be made good at the expense of SUPPLIER, who 
agrees to pay the costs and charges immediately upon demand.
Once an order is placed and confirmation received, the delivery will be accepted at the 
CITY's Water Quality Control Plant 24 hours per day.
12.
Method of Delivery. Delivery shall be by truck tank car. SUPPLIER shall provide all 
unloading equipment including air padding facilities and hoses with appropriate fittings for coupling 
to the CITY's chemical unloading facilities. The unloading facilities have cam and groove couplings; 
exact size and types are available upon request. All shipping containers used shall conform to 
I.C.C. regulations.

the right to inspect any or all of the goods at SUPPLIER’s place of business or upon receipt by the CITY.

Safety Data Sheet. Each bidder must furnish a copy of a completed S.D.S.  (Safety Data Sheet) 
with its bid and annually thereafter. Failure to furnish a completed S.D.S. form will result in rejection 
of the bid. The CITY has a preference for the S.D.S. to be submitted in electronic format. The CITY 
also requires the S.D.S. to be sent to the receiving location if the product supplied is considered to be a 
hazardous substance. All City, State, or Federal requirements for hazardous substances must be strictly 
adhered to.
15.
Training.  SUPPLIER shall provide written instructions regarding the recommended methods 
for cleaning up the chemicals in the event of spills. Such instructions shall include any recommended 
absorbents or chemicals to be used for neutralization, if applicable, and recommended tests to be 
performed, such as pH, to ascertain the effectiveness of the neutralization. These instructions will 
be used by Water Quality Control Plant personnel in cleaning up small spills associated with leaks in 
pumps, valves and other appurtenances.
SUPPLIER shall provide, upon demand of the CITY, two (2) sessions annually, encompassing a 
minimum of two hours per session, of classroom instruction at the Water Quality Control Plant concerning 
manufacture, chemical properties, transport, safe handling and use of the chemicals being provided by 
SUPPLIER. SUPPLIER shall provide for all costs associated with this training.
SUPPLIER will obtain all required federal, state, or local licenses or permits for the transport 
of Sodium Hypochlorite.

immediately give written notice thereof to SUPPLIER and SUPPLIER's sureties, and the sureties shall 
have the right to take over and perform the agreement, provided, however, that if the sureties, within 
5 days after giving them said notice of termination, do not give CITY written notice of their intention to 
take over the performance thereof within 5 days after notice to CITY of such election, CITY may take 
over the work and prosecute the same to completion, by contract or by any other method it may deem 
advisable, for the account, and at the expense of SUPPLIER, and the sureties shall be liable to CITY 
for any excess cost or damages occasioned CITY thereby; and, in such event, CITY may, without liability 
for so doing, take possession of, and utilize in completing the work or supply of goods such materials, 
appliances, plant and other property belonging to SUPPLIER as may be on the site of the work and 
necessary therefor.

Other Insurance Provisions
The insurance policies are to contain, or be endorsed to contain, the following provisions:
Additional Insured Status
The City of San Mateo and The Estero Municipal Improvement District, their elected and 
appointed officials, employees, and agents are to be covered as insureds on the auto policy for 
liability arising out of automobiles owned, leased, hired or borrowed by or on behalf of the 
SUPPLIER; and on the CGL policy with respect to liability arising out of work or operations 
performed by or on behalf of the SUPPLIER including materials, parts or equipment furnished in 
connection with such work or operations. General liability coverage can be provided in the form of an 
endorsement to the SUPPLIER's insurance (at least as broad as ISO Form CG 20 10, 11 85 or both 
CG 20 10 and CG 20 37 forms if later revisions used).
Primary Coverage
For any claims related to this contract, the SUPPLIER’s insurance coverage shall be primary 
insurance as respects the City of San Mateo and The Estero Municipal Improvement District, their elected 
and appointed officials, employees, and agents. Any insurance or self-insurance maintained by the CITY, 
its elected and appointed officials, employees, or agents shall be excess of the SUPPLIER’s insurance and 
shall not contribute with it.
Notice of Cancellation
Each insurance policy required above shall provide that coverage shall not be canceled, except 
after thirty (30) days’ prior written notice (10 days for non-payment) has been given to the CITY.

Any deductibles or self-insured retentions must be declared to and approved by the CITY. The CITY

retention.

otherwise acceptable to the CITY.

SUPPLIER shall furnish the CITY with original certificates and amendatory endorsements or 
copies of the applicable policy language effecting coverage required by this clause. All certificates 
and endorsements are to be received and approved by the CITY before work or performance of 
the contract commences. However, failure to obtain the required documents prior to the work 
beginning shall not waive the SUPPLIER’s obligation to provide them. The CITY reserves the

endorsements required by these specifications, at any time.

22.
Attorney Fees. Attorney fees in amount not exceeding $85 per hour per attorney, and in 
total amount not exceeding $5,000 shall be recoverable as costs (that is, by the filing of a cost 
bill) by prevailing party in any action or actions to enforce the provisions of this Contract. The 
above $5,000 limit is the total of attorney fees recoverable whether in the trial court, appellate court, 
or otherwise, and regardless of the number of attorneys, trials, appeals or actions. It is the intent 
that neither party to this contract shall have to pay the other more than $5,000 for attorney fees 
arising out of an action, or actions, to enforce the provisions of this Contract. The parties expect 
and hope there will be no litigation and that any differences will be resolved amicably.
23.
Mediation. Should any dispute arise out of this Agreement, any party may request that it be 
submitted to mediation. The parties shall meet in mediation within 30 days of a request. The 
mediator shall be agreed to by the mediating parties; in the absence of an agreement, the parties 
shall each submit one name from mediators listed by either the American Arbitration Association, 
the California State Board of Mediation and Conciliation, or other agreed-upon service. The 
mediator shall be selected by a "blindfolded" process.
The cost of mediation shall be borne equally by the parties. Neither party shall be deemed 
the prevailing party. No party shall be permitted to file a legal action without first meeting in 
mediation and making a good faith attempt to reach a mediated settlement. The mediation 
process, once commenced by a meeting with the mediator, shall last until agreement is reached 
by the parties but not more than 60 days, unless the maximum time is extended by the parties.
24.
Governing Law and Venue. This Contract between the parties evidenced hereby or attached 
thereto shall be deemed to be made in the State of California and shall in all respects be construed 
and governed by the laws of that state. In the event of litigation, venue will be in the County of San 
Mateo.
25.
Provisions Cumulative. The provisions of this agreement are cumulative, and in addition 
to and not in limitation of, any other rights or remedies available to CITY.

FROM UNIVAR SOLUTIONS USA LLC
JULY 1, 2025 TO JUNE 30, 2026
THIS PURCHASE AGREEMENT (“Agreement” or “Contract”), made and entered 
into this
               , by and between the CITY OF SAN MATEO, a 
municipal corporation existing under the laws of the State of California, hereinafter referred to 
as "CITY", and U n i v a r  S o l u t i o n s  U S A  L L C . , a corporation, with offices at 
8201 S. 212th, Kent, WA 98032, hereinafter referred to as "SUPPLIER".
R E C I T A L S:
(A) City has taken appropriate proceedings to authorize the purchase of certain goods, 
hereinafter described, and the execution of this Contract.
(B) A notice was duly published for bids for the Contract for the purchase of 
goods hereinafter described.
(C) After notice duly given on the date hereof, the CITY awarded the contract for 
the purchase of goods hereinafter described to SUPPLIER.
IT IS AGREED, as follows:
1.
Scope of Work.
SUPPLIER shall perform the work as described: SUPPLY OF 
UP TO 180,000 GALLONS OF SODIUM BISULFITE 25% SOLUTION.
2.
Contract Price.  CITY shall pay, the SUPPLIER shall accept in full, payment for 
the work above, agreed to the sum not to exceed $285,134.63 (two hundred eighty-five 
thousand one hundred thirty-four dollars and sixty-three cents), priced at $1.445 per gallon of 
Sodium Bisulfite 25% solution, and inclusive of 9.625% sales tax.
Said price is determined by the prices contained in the Bay Area Chemical Consortium 
(BACC) Bid Tabulation for Bid No. 11-2025, Supply and Delivery of Sodium Bisulfite 25% 
Solution. In the event work is performed or materials furnished in addition to or a reduction of 
those set forth in SUPPLIER's bid and the specifications herein, such work and materials will be 
paid for as described in this Agreement.
3.
The Contract Documents. The complete Contract consists of the following 
documents: This Contract and the BACC Bid Tabulation.
All rights and obligations of CITY and SUPPLIER are fully set forth and described 
in the Contract documents.
All of the above-named documents are intended to cooperate, so that any work called 
for in one, and not mentioned in the other, or vice versa, is to be executed the same as if 
mentioned in all said documents.  The documents comprising the complete Contract will 
hereinafter be referred to as "the Contract documents." In the event of any variation or 
discrepancy between any portion of this Contract and any portion of the other Contract 
documents, this Contract shall prevail.
302 of 1111

--- page 303 ---
4.
Termination. This Contract may be terminated by mutual consent of both parties or by 
the CITY immediately in the event of SUPPLIER’s default or breach of the Contract and upon 
60 days written notice without cause. Upon receipt of this written notice, SUPPLIER shall 
stop performance under this Contract as directed by the CITY. If the Contract is terminated, 
SUPPLIER shall be paid in accordance with the terms of the Contract for goods delivered and 
accepted.
5.
Schedule. This is a one-year contract and will cover the period between July 1, 
2025, and June 30, 2026.
6.
Measurement of Quantities, Material Analysis and Identification. SUPPLIER shall 
furnish the Water Quality Control Plant Manager with a legible copy of a Bill of Lading, 
Certified Analysis indicating SODIUM BISULFITE, Weighmaster's certificate of weight and 
any applicable regulatory documents. Certificates shall be delivered to the Water Quality 
Control Plant Manager's designee at the job site upon delivery and before offloading of the 
material.
7.
Payment. Shall be made on a thirty (30) day net basis upon receipt of monthly invoices.
8.
General. Except as directed otherwise in these specifications, full compensation for 
completing all of the supplied goods indicated herein is considered to be included in the Contract 
unit prices paid for the various items of work and no separate payment will be made therefore.
9.
Partial and Final Payments. SUPPLIER shall submit individual invoice for each 
delivery of SODIUM BISULFITE to the City of San Mateo, Water Quality Control Plant, 2050 
Detroit Drive, San Mateo, CA 94404. The invoice shall contain the following information:
a) Purchase order number
b) Date of delivery
c) Item description
d) Cost per gallon and total price
e) Payment terms to include any prompt payment discount offered
f)
All applicable sales tax
10.
General Responsibilities. SUPPLIER shall keep fully informed of all existing, adopted 
and amended federal, state and local laws, ordinances, regulations and orders and decrees which 
in any manner affect those engaged or employed by SUPPLIER in the work, or the equipment 
used by the SUPPLIER, or which in any manner affect the conduct of the work by SUPPLIER, 
and all such orders and decrees of bodies or tribunals having any jurisdiction or authority over 
the same. If any discrepancy or inconsistency is discovered in any such law, ordinance, 
regulation, order or decree, SUPPLIER shall forthwith report the same to the CITY in writing. 
SUPPLIER shall at all times observe and comply with all such existing, adopted and amended 
laws, ordinances, regulations and orders.
SUPPLIER will be required to furnish and update, as necessary, a list of telephone 
numbers and names of responsible parties to be called on a 24-hour, 7 days per week basis 
in the event of an emergency or unusual operating condition.
SUPPLIER assumes full responsibility for all transportation, transportation scheduling, 
packing, handling, insurance, and other services associated with delivery of all products. No 
charges for transportation containers, packing, etc., will be allowed the SUPPLIER unless so 
specified in this Contract.
11.
Time of Delivery.  SODIUM BISULFITE will be furnished F.O.B.  Destination City of 
303 of 1111

--- page 304 ---
San Mateo Water Quality Control Plant 2050 Detroit Drive, San Mateo, CA (i.e., 
Seller/SUPPLIER pays freight charges, owns goods in transit and files any damage claims). 
Deliveries will be made in 3,000-gallon minimum and 5,000-gallon maximum loads.
If SUPPLIER is located outside the 650 area code, then it is requested to provide a 
toll-free telephone number for placing orders and an email address to be used by the CITY to 
send confirmation of order.
Deliveries of SODIUM BISULFITE will generally be prescheduled to provide the 
SUPPLIER with at least three (3) days’ notice before delivery must be made, but the 
SUPPLIER must have the ability after notification to deliver the chemical within 24 hours.
Orders will be placed by phone and confirmed by email. SUPPLIER must email a 
confirmation of the CITY's order within twenty-four (24) hours, unless the CITY requires delivery 
within twenty-four (24) hours then confirmation must be made within three (3) hours. In the event 
SUPPLIER fails to provide the chemicals within twenty-four (24) hours or other scheduled delivery 
timeframe and the CITY is forced to obtain the chemical from an alternative source, all additional 
CITY cost incurred will be made good at the expense of SUPPLIER, who agrees to pay the costs 
and charges immediately upon demand.
Once a n  order is placed and confirmation received, the delivery will be accepted at 
the CITY's Water Quality Control Plant 24 hours per day.
12.
Method of Delivery. Delivery shall be by truck tank car. SUPPLIER shall provide 
all unloading equipment including air padding facilities and hoses with appropriate fittings 
for coupling to the CITY's chemical unloading facilities. The unloading facilities have cam and 
groove couplings; exact size and types are available upon request. All shipping containers 
used shall conform to I.C.C. regulations.
13.
Rejection.  All shipments of SODIUM BISULFITE will be rejected without the 
following:
a) Bill of Lading
b) Certified Analysis including Total Reducing Substance (TRS) as 100% Sodium 
Bisulfite
c) Weighmaster's certificate of weight
d) Applicable regulatory documents
304 of 1111

--- page 305 ---
The CITY may revise shipping instructions as to any goods not as yet shipped. The CITY shall 
have the right to inspect any or all of the goods at SUPPLIER’s place of business or upon receipt 
by the CITY. By reason of its failure to inspect the goods, the CITY shall not be deemed to have 
accepted any defective goods or goods which do not conform to the specifications provided or to 
have waived any of the CITY’s rights or remedies arising by virtue of such defects or non-
c o n f o r m a n c e . SUPPLIER shall be responsible for payment of shipping for the return 
of any defective goods. Shipping documents and invoices must cite the Purchase Order number.
14.
Safety Data Sheet. Each bidder must furnish a copy of a completed S.D.S.  (Safety Data 
Sheet) with its bid and annually thereafter. Failure to furnish a completed S.D.S. form will 
result in rejection of the bid. The CITY has a preference for the S.D.S. to be submitted in 
electronic format. The CITY also requires the S.D.S. to be sent to the receiving location if the 
product supplied is considered to be a hazardous substance. All City, State, or Federal 
requirements for hazardous substances must be strictly adhered to.
15.
Training. SUPPLIER shall provide written instructions regarding the recommended 
methods for cleaning up the chemicals in the event of spills. Such instructions shall include 
any recommended absorbents or chemicals to be used for neutralization, if applicable, and 
recommended tests to be performed, such as pH, to ascertain the effectiveness of the 
neutralization. These instructions will be used by Water Quality Control Plant personnel in 
cleaning up small spills associated with leaks in pumps, valves and other appurtenances.
SUPPLIER shall provide, upon demand of the CITY, two (2) sessions annually, 
encompassing a minimum of two hours per session, of classroom instruction at the Water 
Quality Control Plant concerning manufacture, chemical properties, transport, safe handling and 
use of the chemicals being provided by SUPPLIER. SUPPLIER shall provide for all costs 
associated with this training.
SUPPLIER will obtain all required federal, state, or local licenses or permits for the 
transport of Sodium Bisulfite.
16.
Performance by Sureties. In the event of any termination as hereinbefore provided, 
CITY shall immediately give written notice thereof to SUPPLIER and SUPPLIER's sureties, 
and the sureties shall have the right to take over and perform the Contract,  provided, however, 
that if the sureties, within 5 days after giving them said notice of termination, do not give 
CITY written notice of their intention to take over the performance thereof within 5 days after 
notice to CITY of such election, CITY may take over the work and prosecute the same to 
completion, by contract or by any other method it may deem advisable, for  the account, and at 
the expense of SUPPLIER, and the sureties shall be liable to CITY for any excess cost or 
damages occasioned CITY thereby; and, in such event, CITY may, without liability for so 
doing, take possession of, and utilize in completing the work or supply of goods, such 
materials, appliances, plant and  other property belonging to SUPPLIER as may be on the site 
of the work and necessary therefor.
17.
Insurance.
Coverage shall be at least as broad as:
305 of 1111

--- page 306 ---
1.
Commercial General Liability (CGL): Insurance Services Office (ISO) Form 
CG 00 01 12 07 covering CGL on an “occurrence” basis, including products- 
completed operations, personal & advertising injury, with limits no less than
$2,000,000 per occurrence. If a general aggregate limit applies, either the general 
aggregate limit shall apply separately to this project/location or the general 
aggregate limit shall be twice the required occurrence limit.
2.
Automobile Liability: ISO Form Number CA 00 01 covering any auto (Code 1), 
or if Contractor has no owned autos, hired, (Code 8) and non-owned autos (Code 
9), with limit no less than $1,000,000 per accident for bodily injury and property 
damage.
3.
Workers’ Compensation: as required by the State of California, with Statutory 
Limits, and Employer’s Liability Insurance with limit of no less than $1,000,000 
per accident for bodily injury or disease.
4.
Contractors Pollution Liability and/or Asbestos Pollution Liability and/or 
Errors & Omissions applicable to the work being performed, with a limit no less 
than $2,000,000 per claim or occurrence and $2,000,000 aggregate per policy 
period of one year.
If the SUPPLIER maintains higher limits than the minimums shown above, the CITY 
requires and shall be entitled to coverage for the higher limits maintained by the SUPPLIER.
Other Insurance Provisions
The insurance policies are to contain, or be endorsed to contain, the following provisions:
Additional Insured Status
The City of San Mateo and The Estero Municipal Improvement District, their elected 
and appointed officials, employees, and agents are to be covered as insureds on the auto 
policy for liability arising out of automobiles owned, leased, hired or borrowed by or on 
behalf of the SUPPLIER; and on the CGL policy with respect to liability arising out of work 
or operations performed by or on behalf of the SUPPLIER including materials, parts or 
equipment furnished in connection with such work or operations. General liability coverage 
can be provided in the form of an endorsement to the SUPPLIER’s insurance (at least as 
broad as ISO Form CG 20 10, 11 85 or both CG 20 10 and CG 20 37 forms if later revisions 
used).
Primary Coverage
For any claims related to this Contract, the SUPPLIER’s insurance coverage shall be 
primary insurance as respects the City of San Mateo and The Estero Municipal Improvement 
District, their elected and appointed officials, employees, and agents. Any insurance or self- 
insurance maintained by the CITY, its elected and appointed officials, employees, or agents shall 
be excess of the SUPPLIER’s insurance and shall not contribute with it.
306 of 1111

--- page 307 ---
Notice of Cancellation
Each insurance policy required above shall provide that coverage shall not be canceled, 
except after thirty (30) days’ prior written notice (10 days for non-payment) has been given to 
the CITY.
Waiver of Subrogation
SUPPLIER hereby grants to CITY a waiver of any right to subrogation which any insurer 
of said SUPPLIER may acquire against the CITY by virtue of the payment of any loss under 
such insurance. SUPPLIER agrees to obtain any endorsement that may be necessary to effect 
this waiver of subrogation, but this provision applies regardless of whether or not the CITY has 
received a waiver of subrogation endorsement from the insurer.
Deductibles and Self-Insured Retentions
Any deductibles or self-insured retentions must be declared to and approved by the 
CITY. The CITY may require the SUPPLIER to purchase coverage with a lower deductible or 
retention or provide proof of ability to pay losses and related investigations,  claim administration, 
and defense expenses within the retention.
Acceptability of Insurers
Insurance is to be placed with insurers with a current A.M. Best’s rating of no less than 
A:VII, unless otherwise acceptable to the CITY.
Verification of Coverage
SUPPLIER shall furnish the CITY with original certificates and amendatory 
endorsements or copies of the applicable policy language effecting coverage required by this 
clause. All certificates and endorsements are to be received and approved by the CITY before 
work commences or performance of the Contract commences. However, failure to obtain the 
required documents prior to the work beginning shall not waive the SUPPLIER’s obligation to 
provide them. The CITY reserves the right to require complete, certified copies of all required 
insurance policies, including endorsements required by these specifications, at any time.
18.
Warranties. In addition to any other expressed or implied warranties and unless otherwise 
agreed in writing, SUPPLIER warrants that all products delivered hereunder will be  new, suitable 
for use as described, of the grade and quality specified, free from all defects in design, material 
and workmanship; in conformity with all samples, drawings, descriptions and specifications 
furnished; in compliance with all applicable federal, state and local laws and regulations, 
and free of any liens and encumbrances. These warranties shall not be deemed to exclude 
SUPPLIER’s standard warranties or other rights or warranties which the CITY may have or 
obtain.
19.
Hold Harmless and Indemnity Provision. SUPPLIER agrees to hold harmless and 
indemnify City of San Mateo and Estero Municipal Improvement District, their elected and 
appointed officials,  employees,  and   agents  from  and  against   any   and   all  claims,   loss,
307 of 1111

--- page 308 ---
liability, damage, and expense arising from performance of this  Contract, including claims, 
loss, liability, damage, and expense caused or claimed to be caused by passive negligence of 
City of San Mateo or Estero Municipal Improvement District, their elected and appointed 
officials, employees, and agents; provided, however that this provision does not apply  to claims, 
loss, liability, damage or expense arising from the sole negligence or willful misconduct of City 
of San Mateo or Estero Municipal Improvement District. SUPPLIER will defend City of San 
Mateo and Estero Municipal Improvement District, their elected and appointed officials, 
employees, and agents, against any such claims.
20.
Remedies. In the event of SUPPLIER’s breach of this Contract, CITY may take any or 
all of the following actions, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies available to the 
CITY by law: (a) require SUPPLIER to repair or replace such goods, and upon SUPPLIER’s 
failure or refusal to do so, repair or replace the same at SUPPLIER’s expense; (b) reject any 
shipment or delivery containing defective or nonconforming goods and return for credit or 
replacement at SUPPLIER’s option, said return to be made at SUPPLIER’s cost and risk; (c) 
cancel any outstanding deliveries and treat such breach by SUPPLIER as SUPPLIER’s 
repudiation of this contract. In the event of the CITY’s breach hereunder, SUPPLIER’s exclusive 
remedy shall be SUPPLIER’s recovery of the goods or the purchase price payable for goods 
shipped prior to such breach.
21.
Assignment. SUPPLIER shall not delegate or subcontract any duties and services or 
assign any rights or claims under this Contract without the CITY’s prior written consent.
22.
Attorney Fees. Attorney fees in amount not exceeding $85 per hour per attorney, and 
in total amount not exceeding $5,000 shall be recoverable as costs (that is, by the filing of a 
cost bill) by prevailing party in any action or actions to enforce the provisions of this Contract. 
The above $5,000 limit is the total of attorney fees recoverable whether in the trial court, 
appellate court, or otherwise, and regardless of the number of attorneys, trials, appeals or 
actions. It is the intent that neither party to this contract shall have to pay the other more 
than $5,000 for attorney fees arising out of an action, or actions, to enforce the provisions 
of this Contract. The parties expect and hope there will be no litigation and that  any differences 
will be  resolved amicably.
23.
Mediation. Should any dispute arise out of this Contract, any party may request that it 
be submitted to mediation. The parties shall meet in mediation within 30 days of a request. 
The mediator shall be agreed to by the mediating parties; in the absence of a contract, the 
parties shall each submit one name from mediators listed by either the American Arbitration 
Association, the California State Board of Mediation and Conciliation, or other agreed-upon 
service. The mediator shall be selected by a "blindfolded" process.
The cost of mediation shall be borne equally by the parties. Neither party shall be 
deemed the prevailing party. No party shall be permitted to file a legal action without first 
meeting in mediation and making a good faith attempt to reach a mediated settlement. The 
mediation process, once commenced by a meeting with the mediator, shall last until contract 
is reached by the parties but not more than 60 days, unless the maximum time is extended 
by the parties.
308 of 1111

--- page 309 ---
24.
Governing Law and Venue. This Contract between the parties evidenced hereby 
or attached thereto shall be deemed to be made in the State of California and shall in all 
respects be construed and governed by the laws of that state. In the event of litigation, venue 
will be in the County of San Mateo.
25.
Provisions   Cumulative.
The  provisions   of  this  contract   are  cumulative,   and  
in addition to and not in limitation of, any other rights or remedies available to CITY.
26.
Notices.
All notices  shall  be in  writing,  and  delivered  in person  or transmitted 
by certified mail, postage prepaid.
Notices required to be given to City shall be addressed as follows: 
Director of Public Works
City of San Mateo
330 West 20th 
Avenue San Mateo, 
CA 94403
Notices required to be given to SUPPLIER shall be addressed as appears in 
the signature block as shown on the Bidder's Statement.
27.
Interpretation.  As used herein any gender includes each other gender, the 
singular includes the plural and vice versa.
This document represents the entire and integrated agreement between CITY and 
SUPPLIER and supersedes all prior negotiations, representations, and agreements, either 
written or oral.
This document may be amended only by written instrument, signed by both CITY and 
SUPPLIER.
